- Reducing Belly Fat with a Low-Carb Polyphenol-Rich Diet: Kind: captions Language: en-US Deep belly fat, or visceral fat, that is&nbsp; stored around the abdominal organs is linked&nbsp;&nbsp; to significant health risks, including heart&nbsp; disease, type 2 diabetes, and certain cancers. There are proven strategies&nbsp; for reducing belly fat. One&nbsp;&nbsp; that I have covered extensively&nbsp; on my channel is a low-carb diet. Another strategy is to boost&nbsp; your intake of polyphenols. But here is the thing: Polyphenols come&nbsp; from plants, and because plants make&nbsp;&nbsp; carbohydrates through photosynthesis, boosting&nbsp; polyphenols means you boost your carb intake. Is there a way to marry these two strategies?&nbsp; In other words, can you enjoy your polyphenols&nbsp;&nbsp; and have your low-carb diet benefits, too?&nbsp; Absolutely, and not only is it enjoyable&nbsp;&nbsp; because of the great foods you get to eat,&nbsp; it is also easy to follow and effective. This video shares how polyphenols help to reduce&nbsp;&nbsp; belly fat and the low-carb&nbsp; foods that contain them. [Intro Music] Before I start, let me take one minute&nbsp; to thank LMNT for sponsoring this video. Low-carb diets work for fat loss because they&nbsp; keep the fat-storing hormone insulin low.&nbsp; When insulin is low, you also lose sodium more&nbsp;&nbsp; rapidly. That is why people who eat a&nbsp; whole-food, low-carb diet need salt. We have been taught to avoid salt or sodium.&nbsp; However, sodium is an essential nutrient,&nbsp;&nbsp; meaning your body can't make it, so it must&nbsp; come in through your diet. Sodium is needed&nbsp;&nbsp; to conduct nerve impulses, contract and relax&nbsp; muscles, and maintain proper hydration. When it&nbsp;&nbsp; is low, it can lead to fatigue, muscle&nbsp; cramps, headaches, and irritability. You can get sodium from low-carb&nbsp; foods like pickles, olives,&nbsp;&nbsp; or just the salt shaker. Or you can&nbsp; do what I do and use LMNT every day. LMNT not only tastes great but also&nbsp; contains magnesium and potassium,&nbsp;&nbsp; two additional essential nutrients needed&nbsp; for proper nerve and muscle function. When you use my link, drinklmnt.com/drbecky,&nbsp; to make any purchase, you will receive a free&nbsp;&nbsp; sample pack with eight great flavors. I will&nbsp; leave a link in the description area below. Unlike subcutaneous fat that you can&nbsp; pinch under your skin, the deeper visceral&nbsp;&nbsp; fat is metabolically active and secretes&nbsp; pro-inflammatory cytokines and adipokines. The resulting low-grade, chronic inflammation&nbsp; contributes to insulin resistance,&nbsp;&nbsp; a condition where cells in the body&nbsp; become less responsive to insulin.&nbsp; This resistance impairs glucose&nbsp; uptake, leading to elevated blood&nbsp;&nbsp; sugar levels and promoting fat storage,&nbsp; particularly in the abdominal region. So, you see a vicious cycle emerge&nbsp; where the accumulation of belly fat&nbsp;&nbsp; releases substances that exacerbate inflammation.&nbsp;&nbsp; That inflammation contributes to insulin&nbsp; resistance, which promotes fat storage. Polyphenols are known for their&nbsp; antioxidant properties, so they have&nbsp;&nbsp; powerful anti-inflammatory effects that break&nbsp; that vicious cycle and help reduce belly fat. They also improve metabolic&nbsp; health by enhancing fat oxidation,&nbsp;&nbsp; which is the process of breaking down&nbsp; fatty acids for energy. This enhanced&nbsp;&nbsp; fat breakdown leads to improved energy&nbsp; expenditure and reduced fat storage. Polyphenol-rich foods also suppress&nbsp; appetite by inhibiting the secretion&nbsp;&nbsp; of appetite-stimulating hormones, like&nbsp; ghrelin, and increasing adiponectin levels,&nbsp;&nbsp; furthering this hunger satisfaction effect&nbsp; thanks to the influence adiponectin has on&nbsp;&nbsp; chemical messengers in the hypothalamus&nbsp; that regulate hunger and satiety. Adiponectin also improves insulin sensitivity,&nbsp;&nbsp; allowing cells to better utilize&nbsp; glucose for energy. This reduces&nbsp;&nbsp; blood sugar levels and decreases the storage&nbsp; of fat, particularly in the abdominal area. So, there are a lot of wins when it comes&nbsp; to reducing belly fat by eating polyphenols.&nbsp;&nbsp; And best yet, the foods taste good, and&nbsp; many are naturally low in carbohydrates. Now, to this point in the video,&nbsp; I have talked about polyphenols,&nbsp;&nbsp; which sounds like a single&nbsp; substance found in food. Actually,&nbsp;&nbsp; the term polyphenol is a general term&nbsp; used to describe a class of compounds. There are literally thousands of&nbsp; them, so to reap the full benefits,&nbsp;&nbsp; it is best to incorporate a variety of&nbsp; polyphenol-rich foods into your diet.&nbsp;&nbsp; Let's look at some great low-carb&nbsp; sources, starting with berries. Not all fruits are appropriate for low-carb&nbsp; dieters, but berries, in general, are OK,&nbsp;&nbsp; and their vibrant colors tell&nbsp; us they are rich in polyphenols. We have all heard it said that if you want to ward&nbsp; off illness, you should eat a rainbow every day,&nbsp;&nbsp; which is a catchy way of reminding us to eat&nbsp; a variety of colorful fruits and vegetables.&nbsp;&nbsp; What you are really being told to do is&nbsp; eat your polyphenols every day because&nbsp;&nbsp; many of those rich colors come from these&nbsp; naturally occurring chemical compounds. There are differences among berries. Strawberries&nbsp; are a good low-carb choice because they are widely&nbsp;&nbsp; liked, and a 3.5-ounce (100g) serving contains&nbsp; just 7.7 grams of carbs and 2 grams of fiber.&nbsp;&nbsp; You can also choose blackberries or&nbsp; raspberries. Blueberries are a bit&nbsp;&nbsp; more middle of the range when&nbsp; it comes to their carbohydrate&nbsp;&nbsp; content. We see that a serving of&nbsp; blueberries has 14.5g of carbs. Other fruits high in polyphenols that can be&nbsp; worked into a low-carb diet include plums and&nbsp;&nbsp; apples. You will also find a rich supply&nbsp; of polyphenols in cherries, pomegranates,&nbsp;&nbsp; and grapes, but these fruits have higher&nbsp; carb counts. For the full picture on fruits,&nbsp;&nbsp; see my video on the best and&nbsp; worst low-carb fruit options. Vegetables rich in polyphenols include&nbsp; spinach, broccoli, Brussels sprouts,&nbsp;&nbsp; red onions, asparagus, and artichokes.&nbsp; These are all non-starchy vegetables,&nbsp;&nbsp; so they fit easily into a low-carb diet. To give you an idea, this entire bag of&nbsp; spinach contains just 5 carbohydrates,&nbsp;&nbsp; and you could cook and eat half of this one-pound&nbsp; bag of broccoli and only consume 10 carbs. Carrots are high in polyphenols, which&nbsp; you might guess because of their rich&nbsp;&nbsp; orange color. However, they ride the&nbsp; fence between starchy and non-starchy,&nbsp;&nbsp; so if your goal is to keep your carbs&nbsp; low, limit your intake of carrots. Nuts and seeds are a tasty way to add polyphenols&nbsp; to your daily low-carb diet. The best choices for&nbsp;&nbsp; their polyphenol count are pecans, walnuts,&nbsp; hazelnuts, almonds, flaxseeds, and chia seeds. These foods also provide a great&nbsp; mix of healthy fats and protein,&nbsp;&nbsp; and while being relatively low in&nbsp; carbs, they are easy to overeat. So,&nbsp;&nbsp; do not think of them as a snack food;&nbsp; think of them as a recipe enhancer,&nbsp;&nbsp; so sprinkle chopped pecans, walnuts, or slivered&nbsp; almonds on top of a salad or mix them into&nbsp;&nbsp; full-fat yogurt. You can also add chia seeds&nbsp; and flaxseed to yogurt or a low-carb smoothie. That kind of segways us into beverages.&nbsp; You can drink polyphenols because they&nbsp;&nbsp; are abundant in green tea, coffee, and also&nbsp; red wine. You have heard that the healthy&nbsp;&nbsp; component of red wine is resveratrol.&nbsp; Resveratrol is a type of polyphenol. Other foods that are high in polyphenols&nbsp; and low in carbohydrates are olives,&nbsp;&nbsp; which can be eaten as a snack or&nbsp; included in recipes, and dark chocolate. However, when it comes to dark chocolate,&nbsp;&nbsp; you need to choose wisely. The chocolate&nbsp; needs to be at least 70 percent cacao,&nbsp;&nbsp; preferably 80 percent and above, and it should not&nbsp; have sugar listed in the top three ingredients. That by itself takes some searching,&nbsp; but it is also best to find brands low&nbsp;&nbsp; in lead and cadmium. I will link&nbsp; to the Consumer Reports website&nbsp;&nbsp; where the safer chocolates and those&nbsp; highest in heavy metals are listed. And, when you are preparing food at home,&nbsp; you can give the recipe a polyphenol boost&nbsp;&nbsp; by using olive oil or sprinkling in herbs and&nbsp; spices. Several of them are high in polyphenols,&nbsp;&nbsp; including oregano, thyme, rosemary, basil,&nbsp; parsley, cilantro, cinnamon, and turmeric. And, just to be complete, I will mention that&nbsp; there are a few high-carb foods that are rich in&nbsp;&nbsp; polyphenols. They include grains like oats, rye,&nbsp; barley, and wheat. Beans are another high-carb&nbsp;&nbsp; food with polyphenols, with black beans and&nbsp; red kidney beans having the highest levels. Polyphenols support your goal to lose&nbsp; belly fat by reducing inflammation,&nbsp;&nbsp; enhancing fat oxidation, suppressing&nbsp; appetite, and improving insulin sensitivity. These natural substances are abundant in&nbsp; many low-carb foods, including berries,&nbsp;&nbsp; non-starchy vegetables, nuts and seeds, dark&nbsp; chocolate, olives, olive oil, and herbs. You&nbsp;&nbsp; can also drink polyphenols since they are&nbsp; abundant in green tea, coffee, or red wine. There are thousands of polyphenols&nbsp; that you can take in, so aim to&nbsp;&nbsp; get a variety of these foods into your&nbsp; daily diet for maximum health benefits. I will leave the links to the studies and videos&nbsp; I mentioned in the description area below. I will&nbsp;&nbsp; also leave a link to LMNT along with a link&nbsp; to my 0,1,2,3 strategy that you can download&nbsp;&nbsp; for free if you are looking for a way to get&nbsp; started with a nutrient-dense, low-carb diet&nbsp;&nbsp; that will not take over your life. Thanks for&nbsp; watching and have a great rest of your day.

- 5 Low Carb, High-Fat Foods to Eat Every Day: Kind: captions Language: en A low carb, high-fat diet keeps hunger away and moves your body into a fat burning state. In this video, I'm going to share five lowcarb, high-fat foods that you can easily add to your daily diet. Hi, I'm Dr. Becky from drbeckyfitness.com. I'm a college instructor of the science of nutrition. Let me start with the avocado. Um, avocados contain a lot of calories which can scare people off. Uh, for instance, this avocado weighs just over 200 gram, which means that it contains more than 300 calories, but most of those calories come from healthy monounsaturated fats. And those fats along with um the 13 or more grams of fiber in an avocado satisfy your hunger and they slow down your digestion, which makes it easier for you to consume fewer calories over the course of your day. Um, now I will say that you don't need to eat a whole avocado to reap the benefits. Um, add half of an avocado to a smoothie or uh put it as a salad topper and you'll not only get those healthy fats, you'll also get a healthy dose of vitamins and minerals that will support your metabolism and your overall health. Next, let's go to the much maligned egg. You know, as a society, we are still trying to recover from the years that we were taught that eggs are high in cholesterol and therefore bad for us. Uh while it is true that an egg contains a lot of cholesterol, the notion that eating cholesterol is a main cause of heart disease has been disputed. Um, even the US dietary guidelines that came out in 2015 changed their stance on cholesterol and now say that cholesterol is not considered a nutrient of concern for overconumption. And another study which was published in the same year shows that even people with metabolic issues can eat a high egg diet without consequences. The study shows that people with type 2 diabetes were able to eat 12 eggs a week with no significant change to their total cholesterol, LDL cholesterol, or triglycerides compared to a group that ate just two eggs a week. So, the takeaway is that you can feel free to have at least an egg a day. When you do, you'll get a whole host of micronutrients, including choline, which uh will help with brain function. So, it combats things like brain fog and memory issues. Um, and an egg a day will also keep hunger away. Uh, and that's due to the great mix of proteins and fats that you get from eggs. Um, of course, they're also very versatile, right? So, you can fry them up for breakfast. You can make them into a kiche for dinner, which is something that we often do in in our home. Uh, and hard-boiled eggs make life on a low carb diet easy because they travel well. So, you can carry a hard-boiled egg with you to work for a quick high-fat, low carb afternoon snack. All right, so let's go to walnuts. Now, one of the things I like about walnuts is that they are naturally eaten raw. I recommend all nuts and seeds be eaten raw because when they're roasted, they're typically roasted in an unhealthy vegetable or soybean oil, which wipes out a lot of their benefits. And I'll talk a little bit more about that when I get into the next food. Uh but to stay focused on walnuts about you know 10 12 walnut halves which is about 28 grams of walnuts is all you need in your daily diet to get a tremendous dose of omega-3 fatty acids and a unique blend of antioxidants which are those substances that protect your cells from damage. Um, I will add here that all three of these foods I've talked about so far, avocados, eggs, and walnuts, are also um a great source of B vitamins. And B vitamins help your body convert foods that you eat into energy. So, they help you use calories more efficiently uh, and that sustains your energy throughout the day. Uh, as for how to eat walnuts, well, you can certainly grind them up and put them into recipes, but I find that they make an excellent addition to your salad because the fats in uh walnuts help you absorb the fat soluble vitamins from your salad vegetables. Um, and speaking of healthy fats in u salad, that moves me into my next food, which is full fat salad dressing. And I'm including this here because uh when you go on a diet, it is still one of those taboo items. We think salad and we think low fat dressing. However, putting a full fat dressing on your salad is not something you should be afraid of like walnuts. The fat in dressing helps your body absorb fats soluble vitamins um and also other micronutrients like carotenoids which are the antioxidants that we find in uh bright red uh vegetables and and orange and and uh yellow vegetables. Um so when your salad contains healthy fats, your body is able to pull more of the nutrients out of the salad. And I will tell you that if you have never learned how to make a salad that you truly love to eat, then I encourage you to experiment with adding healthy fats to your salad. Uh if you've been following me for a while, you know the one of my 0123 strategies stands for one large salad each day. Having a salad a day is a great way to jumpstart your healthy weight loss, but if you hate salad, you're not going to go very far. Um, add enough healthy fats to your salad and you will never look back. So, go grab a copy of my free 0123 strategy and then make a salad with avocado, hard-boiled egg, uh, walnuts, and a full fat dressing. Easy, enjoyable, effective, right? Okay. But, uh, there is one caveat I want to mention about full fat dressing, and that is that your dressing should contain a healthy fat. Read the ingredients label and avoid ones with vegetable oil and soybean oil. These are unstable fats and they break down during processing and they become toxic to your body. And uh let me let me save you some time here. It is very hard to find a salad dressing in your grocery store without those oils. I use Primal Kitchen salad dressing. You might be able to find this at your local grocery store. If not, you can find it on my Amazon affiliate shop and I will leave links in the description area below for you. This is made with avocado oil, which is a more stable oil and therefore a healthier fat. All right. And lastly, MCT oil. This oil has come into vogue with the ketogenic diet, which is a very low carb diet. Uh the reason it's linked to a ketogenic diet is because of the unique way it's handled by your body. MCT stands for mediumchain triglyceride. It's a high quality fat that comes mostly from coconut oil. Uh it's a great fuel for for not only your body but also your brain. Uh in your body, MCT oil uh MCTs are easily converted to energy. So when you take them in, they're burned for fuel. Uh and very few of them are stored as fat. So you can obviously see why they're uh oil of interest, right? For those of you that don't know, I have a second YouTube channel that I run with my husband. + Key Microbiome Changes from a High-Fat Diet:

1. Decreased microbial diversity:
HFD tends to reduce the overall diversity of gut bacteria, which is often associated with worse metabolic and immune outcomes.


2. Increase in Firmicutes and decrease in Bacteroidetes:
Many studies (especially in rodents) show that HFD increases the ratio of Firmicutes to Bacteroidetes — a shift often linked to obesity and inflammation.


3. Rise in pro-inflammatory bacteria:
HFD can promote the growth of pathobionts (potentially harmful bacteria), such as certain Proteobacteria, which are linked to inflammation and gut permeability (“leaky gut”).


4. Reduced short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) producers:
HFD often decreases bacteria that produce beneficial metabolites like butyrate, which is important for gut barrier function and anti-inflammatory effects.


5. Altered bile acid metabolism:
High fat intake increases bile secretion, which selects for bile-tolerant microbes like Bilophila wadsworthia, linked to inflammation and colitis.




---

Long-term Effects of Microbiome Shifts:

Increased risk of metabolic diseases (e.g., obesity, type 2 diabetes)

Disruption of gut barrier function

Promotion of systemic inflammation

Impaired immune regulation



---

Factors that Influence the Microbiome Shift:

Type of fat (saturated vs. unsaturated)

Duration of the diet

Baseline microbiome composition

Host species (human vs. animal models)


[- fruits vegetables lentils are key for healthy gut and your health(due to soluble fiber)]

  11. @thinkerds: This video discusses how fiber is the only carbohydrate that does not spike insulin (0:25). Insulin is a hormone that promotes fat storage and makes weight loss difficult (0:06). While many fiber-rich foods also contain other carbs that do influence insulin, such as bread and pasta, the best sources of fiber are non-starchy vegetables (1:10).

Key points about fiber and a healthy ketogenic diet:
Non-starchy vegetables should be a significant part of a healthy keto plan, with Dr. Berg recommending 7 to 10 cups per day (1:47). These vegetables do not need to be counted towards daily carbohydrate limits (1:17).
Beyond fiber, vegetables provide essential minerals (like potassium and magnesium), vitamins (C, folate, B vitamins), and phytonutrients (1:57), which have anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory properties (2:24).
The fiber in vegetables acts as food for your gut microbiome, which then produces compounds that help reduce insulin resistance and increase insulin sensitivity (2:30). This process can ultimately aid in weight loss (2:57).

  1. @PatrickMalonee: what helped me most was pairing high protein with my GLP-1 from FormBlends, appetite stayed manageable all day
  2. @Bithi-j6d: the thing people always overlook with high protein eating for weight loss is that not all protein sources do the same job, even at equal gram counts. like 30g of protein from chicken breast and 30g from a processed protein bar technically look the same on a tracker but your body handles them very differently in terms of satiety, thermic effect, and how long until hunger creeps back.

what genuinely surprised me when i started paying more attention to this: cottage cheese and eggs became my most used foods not because they're exciting but because the hunger suppression is noticeably longer than almost anything else i tried at similar calories. greek yogurt is close but i found the fat-free versions didn't hold me as long, the full-fat versions do something different with satiety that the numbers don't fully explain.

the point about fish is worth emphasizing too because people default to chicken breast as the one protein food and kind of forget that salmon and sardines are bringing omega-3s alongside the protein, which matters for inflammation and actually supports the metabolic side of weight loss in ways that go beyond just macros. i've been on compounded tirzepatide through formblends for about five months and one thing i've noticed is that building meals around these specific protein sources made a real difference in how smoothly the appetite suppression works, like it compounds the effect rather than fighting it.

the practical takeaway i'd add for anyone watching: aim to hit at least 25g protein at breakfast specifically, because that single change probably does more for your afternoon cravings than any other single adjustment you can make.

- 10 Low Carb Metabolism Boosting Foods for Thyroid Support: Kind: captions Language: en thyroid hormones regulate your metabolic rate to optimize their performance you need to get the right nutrients inside of you in this video i'll share 10 low carb foods that contain these nutrients so you can eat your way to a better metabolism every cell in your body needs thyroid hormones to help generate and use energy which ultimately regulates your basal metabolic rate or bmr to do their job these metabolic hormones must get inside of your cells which is no small feat because there are three obstacles your body must overcome first your thyroid gland must make a sufficient amount of hormones next the inactive form which is t4 must be converted to the active form which is t3 and then that activated hormone must cross the cell membrane to get inside your cell where it does its job overcoming each of these obstacles requires specific nutrients you can get those nutrients into your diet with these 10 foods first on our list is shrimp i put it first because it is rich in iodine which is one of the principal nutrients needed to make thyroid hormones in fact the full name for the active t3 is triodo thyronine and if you look closely at that name you'll see that the beginning of the word tri-iodo literally means three iodine atoms are within the chemical compound the problem is that our bodies cannot make iodine so we need to get it from our diets we do that by eating iodine rich foods like shrimp or other foods that come from the sea like other kinds of seafood or even seaweed for instance iodized salt is another option for boosting the amount of iodine that you're consuming however shrimp gives you some added thyroid benefits besides iodine it has a good source of selenium which is a mineral that helps to turn the inactive form of thyroid hormones which is that t4 into the active t3 form wild salmon is a type of fish that contains many nutrients that are vital for thyroid function one of those nutrients is tyrosine which is an amino acid like iodine tyrosine is needed for your thyroid gland to make thyroid hormones salmon also provides a healthy dose of selenium which i mentioned helps with the activation of thyroid hormones and it also is rich in omega-3 fatty acids these fatty acids support the integrity of your cell membranes creating a smooth path for thyroid hormones to cross into your cells where they regulate energy production so when you include salmon in your diet you provide the nutrients needed to make activate and deliver thyroid hormones more efficiently spinach and other dark leafy greens are a great source of selenium they also have iron and zinc iron and zinc work alongside selenium to enhance the conversion of t4 to t3 zinc also supports your immune system which may offer additional support for individuals with the most common form of hypothyroidism which is an autoimmune form called hashimoto's thyroiditis spinach can be used as a base of your daily salad or it can be incorporated in a recipe or omelettes or soups and these richly colored leaves also pack a healthy serving of vitamin a which is helpful for optimizing thyroid hormone function bone broth is rich in collagen and the gut healthy amino acids glycine and proline that help to prevent or heal leaky gut a leaky gut also called increased intestinal permeability is a condition that can increase your risk of the autoimmune form of hypothyroidism with leaky gut the cell junctions that line the digestive tract wall spread apart allowing small food particles to leak out of the tract and into the blood these leaked particles provide a path for food proteins like gluten and dairy proteins as well as other digested particles to pass into your bloodstream where your immune system identifies them as foreign invaders that must be attacked because gluten and dairy molecules look a lot like thyroid cells your thyroid cells get attacked in a case of mistaken identity this is a phenomenon known as molecular mimicry and has been identified as playing a role in autoimmune diseases there are different bone broths available in liquid or powder form that easily mixes with any hot or cold drink and the added perk of bone broth is the collagen that it has supports the health of your hair skin and nails but you do need to buy quality the cheap bone broth cartons that you find in the grocery store are likely only simmered for a few hours which is not long enough to extract the collagen and the nutrients that you need to benefit your gut and ultimately your thyroid garlic is often touted for its health benefits and contains nutrients beneficial for optimal thyroid function it is a good source of selenium and it might help your body to metabolize iron a daily dose of garlic will also supply your body with b vitamins needed for the support of your immune system and help to inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines that are involved in hashimoto's thyroiditis walnuts are a great source of plant-based omega-3 fatty acids these healthy fats support your cell membranes allowing thyroid hormones to move into your cells easily walnuts are something that i eat just about every day on my daily salad they provide a nice crunch they have a wonderful mix of macronutrients for low carb dieters even if you just have four or five walnut halves a day which is about a half of an ounce you'll get more than nine grams of healthy fats and less than two grams of carbs and while that half ounce will have 90 calories walnuts are hunger satiating making them an easy enjoyable and effective part of your overall weight loss plan now one small note according to the mayo clinic if you are taking thyroid hormone replacement medication walnuts may impair the absorption of the medication so they should not be consumed together okay next we have blueberries which are filled with the vitamins your body needs to make thyroid hormones and move your hormones into your cells and their deep rich color indicates that they are rich in antioxidants which are compounds that indirectly support thyroid health by reducing inflammation and boosting your immune system now blueberries are not the lowest carb berry but they have a favorable glycemic index of 53 so you can feel good about incorporating them into recipes without fear of a blood sugar spike that can block fat loss and trigger hunger brazil nuts are one of the most concentrated dietary sources of selenium and with only about three grams of carbs per ounce they are a satisfying way to support your thyroid function and brazil nuts as you can see are large in size and just one nut a day will be enough to meet or exceed the recommended dietary allowance for selenium which for adults is 55 micrograms coconut oil contains medium chain triglycerides or mcts that are easily metabolized to provide energy and encourage weight loss and one of the main benefits for thyroid support is the high concentration of lauric acid in coconut oil this particular mct prevents the overgrowth of gut bacteria viruses and fungi that can open the door to thyroid issues some people will stir coconut oil into their coffee but being that it remains stable when heated it is also a very good choice for cooking all right next on my list is one of my favorites which is pumpkin seeds these low carb seeds are a source of tyrosine which is the amino acid that links with iodine to make thyroid hormones being that many sources of tyrosine are animal based foods pumpkin seeds are a viable way for those who don't eat a lot of animal products to boost their tyrosine intake pumpkin seeds also supply iron and selenium which are two of the nutrients needed to convert inactive thyroid hormone to the active form you know when your thyroid hormones are doing their job your metabolism is working for you helping you burn calories and lose weight when you include these 10 low carb foods in your diet you give your body the nutrients it needs to support your thyroid and metabolism and you add to the enjoyment of your low-carb diet i put these foods together in a download for you that you can get from my website for free and i will share the links here on the video and below in the description please join my channel by clicking on the subscribe button and then the bell icon which will alert you when my next video is released thanks so much for watching

I wish I knew before I started eating a higher protein diet that would have made my life and wellness goals and weight loss goals so much easier let's dive into it my name is Autumn I'm a certified clinical nutritionist with my Master's nutrition Human Performance and that first thing is that eating a higher protein diet is not just for bodybuilders a lot of women myself included tended to shy away from a higher protein intake or even just getting enough protein in general because there's this idea that higher protein is really only needed for bodybuilders or that you're going to get bulky if you eat too much protein but boy is this wrong women especially should really be focusing on high quality protein sources because it helps to maintain the metabolism so the metabolism doesn't go down helps to maintain muscle mass and toning and it helps to protect from bone loss ultimately helping to protect from osteoporosis and to hop it all off eating of protein is one of the easiest ways - Starchy vs. Non Starchy Vegetables on a Low Carb Diet: Kind: captions Language: en vegetables are healthy carbs but if you're on a low-carb diet you'll get better results if you pick non starchy vegetables over starchy ones in this video explain how to tell the difference hi I'm dr. Becky from dr. Becky Fitness comm I'm a college instructor of the science of nutrition I have here in front of me two groups of vegetables this group is comprised of non starchy vegetables and this group contains starchy ones starch is a term that describes the carbohydrate molecule if something is a starchy food then it contains a long chain of sugar molecules bonded together so starchy vegetables contain more carbohydrates than their non starchy counterparts and they also tend to be higher on the glycemic index which means that they cause more of a spike in your blood sugar and insulin levels and that is not what you want when you're on a low carb diet for weight loss so you're better off avoiding or limiting starchy vegetables the challenge is that there are obviously many different vegetables so how do you tell which is which well some of it comes down to memorization but there are some tricks that you can use to tell the difference and I will say that these tricks are not hard and fast rules but they will help guide you in the right direction so with that said one thing that you can look at is the density of the vegetables if we pick up a starchy vegetable we notice that it is very dense and heavy for instance a potato and a rutabaga or a Swede as it is referred to outside of the US there were like rocks when they are raw and it's the same with other root vegetables like parsnips right they're solid Hardy root vegetables starchy vegetables are so dense that they feel like you know it just feels like you could use them in a baseball game if you really wanted to now non starchy vegetables on the other hand tend to be light airy and watery so leafy greens collard greens cabbage right they're very light bell peppers are hollow in the middle making them very light so that is a whoo that they are non-starchy in nature now of course there are some exceptions to the density rule and you could argue that cucumbers eggplant zucchinis onions you don't have some heft to them but that heft is mostly due to their high water content so even though they are heavier they're still considered non starchy vegetables now there are a couple of vegetables worth singling out here and those would be carrots and beets or beet roots carrots oh and and beets are very dense yet they are kind of on the fence between starchy vegetables and non starchy vegetables they're not the starchy astir vegetables but they are relatively high on the glycemic index which means that they're not a terrible choice but they're certainly not your best choice when you're on a low-carb diet so with that well we can look at some other factors to help us look at the difference between starchy and non starchy vegetables one of those factors is the time of year the vegetables are harvested fall vegetables tend to be higher in starch and this might be one of those ancestral factors higher starch Foods helped our ancestors pack on pounds so they could survive the winter months when food was scarce so fall vegetables like pumpkin and winter squashes like butternut or acorn squash or starchy vegetables so again that's not a hard fast rule but the the vegetable tends to have a relatively short maturation period like you're outside walking to your garden all summer long long and harvesting that vegetable it's probably more of a non starchy vegetable and that would hold true and we look at examples like our lettuces our cucumbers zucchinis peppers we could throw tomatoes in that mix as well and one more little trick if you haven't quite got the hang of distinguishing between starchy and non starchy vegetables is to think about when you eat them if you live here in the United States and celebrate Thanksgiving we do so with a big Thanksgiving meal if there are vegetables on the table they are more likely to be our starchy ones right our sweet potatoes corn peas butternut squash if we have does we have pumpkin pie non-starchy vegetables on the other hand they don't make it into too many of our large celebratory meals but they go great on a salad so kale spinach broccoli artichoke hearts celery onions these add fiber and flavor to salads but not a lot of carbohydrates and that's why a salad a day is a great way to get your weight loss started in fact it is the one of my zero one two three strategy if you haven't picked up that strategy yet I think it will be beneficial to you I will leave some links here on the video or down in my description area it's perfectly free but it is a great way for you to learn how to get non starchy vegetables into your daily diet and get rid of the biggest diet destroyer which is sugar so grab a copy of that zero one two three strategy to get yourself started thanks for watching I hope this was helpful if it was please subscribe and I will be back soon with another video to help you reach your goal thanks you

- The Lowest and Highest Carb Vegetables are...: Kind: captions Language: en let's talk about the vegetables that have the lowest net carb ratio now when we're talking about net carb we're talking about taking the total carb and minusing the fiber why do we do that because fiber has a zero effect on insulin and blood sugars our bodies cannot digest fiber we feed it to our microbes that then turn it into some some food for our colon cells but fiber does not affect insulin or blood sugars so you don't have to worry about that now in this category right here okay these are all the - I’ve Changed My Diet (Menopause Made Me Do It): Kind: captions Language: en-US For the past eight years, I have been&nbsp; following a low-carb diet and practicing&nbsp;&nbsp; intermittent fasting. I still do those things.&nbsp; But, blame it on menopause or just Father Time,&nbsp;&nbsp; now that I am in my later 50s, I have had&nbsp; to make some modifications to feel my best.&nbsp;&nbsp; This video details the specifics&nbsp; of how my diet has changed and why. [Intro Music] With age comes wisdom, but age&nbsp; also brings some physical changes,&nbsp;&nbsp; and I have noticed the changes&nbsp; in my body that come with age. To be specific, belly fat is&nbsp; a real thing after menopause;&nbsp;&nbsp; eating for health becomes more real with age,&nbsp; and body composition shifts toward fat rather&nbsp;&nbsp; than muscle if you are not watching. Those&nbsp; are the main reasons that I changed my diet. To keep pace with my changing body,&nbsp; I have adjusted my daily and monthly&nbsp;&nbsp; macronutrient ratio, which I will explain.&nbsp; I have also taken steps to support muscle,&nbsp;&nbsp; and I have shifted my snack choices. What has not changed is my focus&nbsp; on the 3Es and eating whole foods. Eating and physical activity should always&nbsp; be looked at as a long-range adjustment,&nbsp;&nbsp; not a quick fix. Long-range adjustments are&nbsp; achievable for anyone when the plan you have&nbsp;&nbsp; meets the 3Es, meaning your plan is easy for&nbsp; you to follow, enjoyable, and effective. When&nbsp;&nbsp; you have those three things in place, there is&nbsp; no need or desire to go back to your old habits. The changes I have made fit my 3Es, and&nbsp; adding GEM to my daily routine has as well. GEM is a real-food multivitamin that provides&nbsp; nutrients your body can readily absorb and use. These GEM bites have become an&nbsp; enjoyable part of my daily routine. It looks like a treat, and it is,&nbsp;&nbsp; but it is a treat that is packed with more&nbsp; than 20 whole food vitamins, minerals,&nbsp;&nbsp; superfoods, prebiotics and probiotics to&nbsp; help fill the gaps in your daily nutrition. On my diet, I eat both plant and animal&nbsp; foods. A flaw is that I am routine,&nbsp;&nbsp; so I tend to eat the same salad and vegetable&nbsp; side dishes each day. GEM gives me a variety&nbsp;&nbsp; of nutrients that I do not get from my typical&nbsp; diet, and I love that about these GEM bites. You can eat a bite any time of day, I&nbsp; enjoy one after lunch. It has a nice tangy,&nbsp;&nbsp; citrus flavor and a really&nbsp; satisfying chewy texture. Right now, is a great time to try these real-food,&nbsp;&nbsp; nutrient-dense bites because GEM&nbsp; is offering a special deal off your&nbsp;&nbsp; first month! Go to dailyGEM.com/DRBECKY&nbsp; and use my code DRBECKY at checkout! The first change I have made involves shifting&nbsp; my macros. I have done this daily to include&nbsp;&nbsp; more protein and monthly by incorporating&nbsp; three-day periods of intermittent carnivore. As we age, one of the biggest threats to quality&nbsp; of life is the loss of muscle and muscle strength.&nbsp;&nbsp; Eating more protein after you reach the age&nbsp; of 50 supports muscle mass preservation,&nbsp;&nbsp; and when paired with resistance&nbsp; exercises that tax your muscles,&nbsp;&nbsp; it preserves muscle strength&nbsp; and encourages growth. You have likely heard that more&nbsp; protein is good as we age. However,&nbsp;&nbsp; what does that look like? It is more&nbsp; simple than you may imagine. For instance,&nbsp;&nbsp; when I started eating low-carb, I&nbsp; followed a 60-20-20 macronutrient ratio,&nbsp;&nbsp; meaning 60 percent of my daily calories came&nbsp; from fat and 20 percent came from both protein&nbsp;&nbsp; and carbohydrates. My diet today is closer to&nbsp; 60-25-15. So, my fat intake has stayed the same,&nbsp;&nbsp; while my protein intake increased by 5 percent&nbsp; and my carb intake decreased by the same amount. A 5 percent increase in daily protein will depend&nbsp; on your calorie intake, but for most, this means&nbsp;&nbsp; adding 15 to 25 grams of protein. For example,&nbsp; I have added a protein shake after I exercise,&nbsp;&nbsp; which boosts my protein intake by 13 grams, and&nbsp; I have added a hard-boiled egg or a couple of&nbsp;&nbsp; ounces of chicken or meat to my daily salad&nbsp; for an additional 6 to 10 grams of protein. I have also incorporated three-day periods of&nbsp; intermittent carnivore into my monthly diet. The Intermittent Carnivore Diet,&nbsp; as I call it, is a diet regimen&nbsp;&nbsp; that introduces brief periods of eating zero&nbsp; carbs or as close to that goal as possible. On a carnivore diet, you eat meat and&nbsp; other animal products. There are no&nbsp;&nbsp; plant-based foods because all&nbsp; plants contain carbohydrates. I love this dieting strategy and utilize it&nbsp; twice a month. I have a video describing what&nbsp;&nbsp; I eat and how I do it. I will leave a link&nbsp; for you if you would like all of the details. To give you an overview for this video,&nbsp; the concept of intermittent carnivore&nbsp;&nbsp; is simple. For three days, you eat only&nbsp; carnivore foods. So, food choices include&nbsp;&nbsp; meats (i.e., beef, bison, fish, lamb, organ meats,&nbsp; pork, poultry, seafood), eggs, bone broth, animal&nbsp;&nbsp; fats (i.e., butter, lard, tallow), and some dairy&nbsp; products (i.e., full-fat milk, cream, cheese). I do not count calories on my carnivore days.&nbsp; One of the nice things you find about the diet&nbsp;&nbsp; is that your food intake is self-limiting.&nbsp; There are not a lot of choices, and the&nbsp;&nbsp; choices that are allowed are high in protein&nbsp; and fat, two very hunger-satisfying nutrients. The second change I have made is&nbsp; adding creatine to my daily routine. Creatine is a supplement that supports&nbsp; muscle growth and strength. I have&nbsp;&nbsp; used it periodically for years, but&nbsp; now I use it as a daily supplement. Creatine came into significance in the&nbsp; body-building world because it helps&nbsp;&nbsp; your muscles produce energy. But creatine&nbsp; supplementation has been shown to act as a&nbsp;&nbsp; possible countermeasure to the menopausal-related&nbsp; decrease in muscle, bone, and strength. And while some research suggests that&nbsp; the supplement alone can improve muscle&nbsp;&nbsp; mass and strength in older adults, the results&nbsp; are best when paired with resistance training. One thing I will note about creatine is that&nbsp; it can increase water retention in muscles.&nbsp;&nbsp; This is a good thing, not a bad one. However,&nbsp;&nbsp; that water retention may show up as a&nbsp; couple of pounds of weight gain when&nbsp;&nbsp; you step on the scale. It does not cause you&nbsp; to gain fat. Quite the opposite, but you may&nbsp;&nbsp; gain weight from the water retention in your&nbsp; muscles initially and muscle growth over time. The third thing that I have changed is my snack&nbsp; choices. I found that I felt better and had an&nbsp;&nbsp; easier time maintaining my weight when I replaced&nbsp; my typical low-carb snacks with protein snacks. When I started eating low-carb, typical snacks&nbsp; included full-fat yogurt with blueberries,&nbsp;&nbsp; raw or toasted nuts, and dark chocolate. Today,&nbsp;&nbsp; I feel my best when I satisfy between-meal-hunger&nbsp; with a beef stick, hard-boiled eggs, or cheese. These trade-offs have helped me achieve&nbsp; my daily macronutrient shift toward more&nbsp;&nbsp; protein and fewer carbs without sacrificing&nbsp; the 3E's. Grabbing a beef stick, boiled egg,&nbsp;&nbsp; or a few slices of cheese is&nbsp; easy, enjoyable, and effective. I like routine as much as anyone, but we&nbsp; live in dynamic bodies that adapt as time&nbsp;&nbsp; passes. If your diet feels stale, you&nbsp; are not progressing toward your goal,&nbsp;&nbsp; or the things that have worked in the past are no&nbsp; longer working, be open to making some changes. Over the past year, I have added more daily&nbsp; protein, incorporated monthly intermittent&nbsp;&nbsp; carnivore days, added creatine, and shifted&nbsp; to protein snacks. These changes have been&nbsp;&nbsp; effective for me, and I hope they give you some&nbsp; ideas that you can incorporate into your life. Are there things that you have&nbsp; shifted in your diet over time? If so,&nbsp;&nbsp; please share them in the comments below.&nbsp; Thanks for watching and thanks to GEM for&nbsp;&nbsp; sponsoring this video. I will leave a&nbsp; link to the GEM bites and the videos&nbsp;&nbsp; I mentioned in the description or more&nbsp; area below. Have a great rest of your day.

- Dieta Low Carb - Wellness Factory Brazil: Kind: captions Language: en Therefore, a diet is not necessary; its primary goal is to prevent the accumulation of fat in adipose tissue and other organs such as the liver and pancreas. Glucose, glycogen, and fatty acids are the main sources of energy for cells. A low-carb diet aims to keep insulin levels low, allowing the body to use energy from burning fat. This occurs because insulin normally transforms excess carbohydrates not used as energy into fat in the form of triglycerides. Increased insulin levels inhibit the pituitary gland, favoring an increase in the size of fat cells and are widely related to obesity. The dietary guideline is that 50 to 55 percent of the daily calorie needs should be carbohydrates. Low-carb diets suggest that carbohydrates can make up between 10 and 40 percent of the calories consumed in a day, between 50 and 150 grams per day, preferably those with a low glycemic index. As carbohydrate consumption decreases, an increase in the proportion of protein and fats is suggested, which gives a feeling of greater satiety and preservation of lean mass. The ketogenic diet is a... The most restrictive type of low-carb diet. The main difference between the two diets is that the ketogenic diet limits carbohydrate consumption to a maximum of 30-50 grams per day, or 5-10 percent of daily calorie needs. The ketogenic diet aims to use fat as the primary energy source in the form of ketone bodies. Therefore, it responds more strongly to carbohydrates compared to the low-carb diet. It is rich in fat, moderate in protein ( around 70% fat, 20-25% protein, and 5-10% carbohydrates). For the treatment of obesity, it is safe to say that balanced and individualized hypocaloric diets, considering lifestyle and dietary preferences, which I propose, along with nutritional re-education and physical activity, promote more effective and lasting results. For more information, consult Welles Nutre. Learn more about our services on the website www.rextur.com.br and subscribe to our YouTube channel and social media.

Instead, we find ourselves drowning in a sea of conflicting advice. This is especially true in the realm of health and wellness. One website touts the benefits of a vegan diet. Another promotes high protein, low carb eating. It's enough to make anyone's head spin. This constant barrage of information creates confusion and distrust. Consumers are left wondering who to believe. The result, people become paralyzed by indecision or worse, they fall prey to fat diets and quick fixes. These rarely deliver lasting results, and they can even be harmful. The wellness industry, unfortunately, profits from this confusion. Expensive supplements and restrictive meal plans promise miraculous transformations. But true wellness is not about deprivation or quick fixes. It's about adopting sustainable habits that support long-term health and well-being. It's about nourishing our bodies with real food and moving them with intention. We need to be critical consumers of online information. We must learn to discern evidence-based advice from marketing hype. By arming ourselves with knowledge, we can take control of our health and well-being. We can navigate the digital maze and find our own path to a healthier, happier life. Our modern food system is a marvel of efficiency and convenience. We can walk into a supermarket at any time of year and find a dazzling array of produce. But this abundance often comes at a cost. Industrial farming practices prioritize profit over health. Pesticides, herbicides, and chemical fertilizers are used liberally. These chemicals find their way into our food and ultimately into our bodies. The long-term effects of these chemicals are still being studied, but there is growing concern about their potential impact on our health. Some studies suggest links to a range of health problems. These include hormone disruption, reproductive issues, and even cancer. The way we store and cook our food can also impact its nutritional value and safety. For example, many plastic containers leech harmful chemicals into food. This is especially true when heated. Improper cooking temperatures can also lead to the formation of harmful compounds. These form in foods like meat. Choosing organic produce can help reduce pesticide exposure. So can washing fruits and vegetables thoroughly before consumption. We also need to be mindful of food storage practices. Glass or stainless steel containers are safer alternatives to plastic. And cooking food thoroughly and at the right temperature is crucial for safety. By being aware of the potential dangers lurking in our food system, we can make informed choices. We can protect our health and the health of our families. Wellness retreats have become synonymous with luxury and indulgence. We envision serene spars, gourmet meals, and daily yoga classes. While these can be enjoyable aspects of a retreat, they often overshadow the true purpose to promote holistic well-being. Many retreats focus on pampering the body but neglect the mind and spirit. This superficial approach to wellness fails to address the root causes of our stress and imbalance. It also makes these retreats inaccessible to most people. The high cost of luxury accommodations, gourmet meals, and specialized treatments excludes a large segment of the population. This creates a system where wellness is a privilege enjoyed by the few, not a right accessible to all. A truly transformative wellness retreat should be grounded in a holistic approach. It should address the physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual dimensions of well-being. This involves incorporating practices like mindfulness, meditation, and personal reflection. These practices help us connect with our inner selves and develop a deeper understanding of our needs. Imagine a retreat that offers a space for introspection and growth. A place where you can disconnect from the demands of daily life and reconnect with your true self. This is the kind of wellness experience that can have a lasting impact long after you return home. We need a new model for wellness, one that is accessible, affordable, and truly transformative. This new paradigm should prioritize honesty, transparency, and integrity. It should be grounded in a deep respect for the interconnectedness of mind, body, and spirit. It should empower individuals to take control of their health and well-being. Imagine a wellness retreat that combines the wisdom of traditional healing practices with the insights of modern science. A place where you can receive personalized guidance from experienced practitioners. A place where you can connect with nature, nourish your body with wholesome food, and engage in activities that bring you joy. This new model of wellness is not about luxury or indulgence. It's about creating a space for healing, growth, and transformation. It's about making wellness a part of everyday life, not just a fleeting experience. This vision requires a shift in our mindset. We need to move away from the quick fix mentality and embrace a more holistic and sustainable approach to health and well-being. We need to create a world where everyone has the opportunity to live a healthier, happier, and more fulfilling life. This is the true promise of wellness. I want to collaborate on this startup idea to enhance lifespan. Whether you're a professional traditional medicine expert or investor, let's talk.

DO NOT trust "cauliflower" crusts implicitly. They usually have starches in them to stick them together.
Cream soups and sauces are made with a lot of corn starch.
The common artificial sweeteners will almost always have 5 grams of dextrose and/or maltodextrin: both of which raise glucose than sucrose or fructose do.
Fast food burgers have enough sugar you can measure the blood glucose rise.

The problem with net carb, is the fiber should be inherently bound up with the starch, not just added as a separate ingredient.
Memorize this: not all carbs are equal. They ain't gonna give you Glycemic Index or Load values.
Finally, about 1/3 of the protein your body doesn't use will likely be turned into glucose by your liver.
Without frequent BUN/Creatinine Ratio tests, you can only guess what you are actually using up. O

Why seed oils are common
	•	Neutral flavor & low cost → Soybean oil, canola oil, and sunflower oil are inexpensive and don’t overpower flavor.
	•	Texture & stability → They emulsify well with water, vinegar, and stabilizers, giving dressings their creamy consistency.
	•	Long shelf life → Refined seed oils resist rancidity, making them ideal for mass-market products.

•	Prioritize & Plan (01:24 - 03:16): Faye uses a 'brain dump' and the Eisenhower Matrix to clear mental clutter and reduce decision fatigue by categorizing tasks based on urgency and importance.

•	Mind Over Matter (06:01 - 07:29): She replaces passive consumption (like watching reality TV) with active learning through platforms like Skillshare to improve cognitive flexibility.

•	Midday Reset (08:40 - 13:15): She incorporates mindfulness and meditation to combat midday energy dips and lower cortisol levels, helping her stay present during stressful or high-stakes moments.
•	Push & Rest (13:16 - 13:59): By tackling her most important tasks early via the Eisenhower Matrix, she can fully 'switch off' in the evening, establishing firm work boundaries to improve sleep and reduce rumination.
•	Quality Time (14:00 - 14:45): Ending the day with focused, meaningful social interaction to boost oxytocin and reconnect with what matters most.

And some might say, what is the point of working so hard when you can't even treat yourself? And to that I say, maybe we're just conditioned to think that splurging on ourselves is a form of self-care. But actual self-care looks like working towards a life that truly enriches us. And maybe these little splurges are distractions along the way. Growing up in Korea, we lived very frugally, but I was still happy as I could be. Now in my mid-30s, I've come full circle and still embrace many of those same habits. These small everyday decisions might seem insignificant on their own, but it does add up over time. But more importantly, I found that living a lowmaintenance lifestyle comes with a piece that outweighs anything I can buy. So, I don't mind skipping the coffee. **If you're doing full body 3x per week or an upper/lower split where you do each one twice per week with four days total, that is more than enough. High intensity with only 1-2 reps shy of failure and moderate volume.

2) If you're training legs 2-3x per week as part of a full body or an upper/lower with compound lifts, the sled, etc you're getting plenty of cardio as is. 

**Daily brisk walking is a great counterpart to lifting over formal zone 2 cardio and sprints. It won't impede lifting, speeds up recovery, and can be done every day year round. Stacking 10-15K steps over  each day will yield serious calorie burn long-term.

And I'll tell you what my system is right now so you can copy it. You start with a three-year vision. I think one of the best books around creating a vision for yourself is called Vivid Vision by Cameron Herald. And he takes you through the step on how to create that three-year vision. Once you set that three-year vision, you're going to break it down into a oneyear goal. You're going to ask yourself, what do I have to do this year in order to get myself closer to the vision? Then you're going to break that one-year goal down into quarterly projects. And those quarterlys are broke down into monthly tasks. And those tasks are broken into weekly tasks. And those tasks are scheduled inside of my calendar to be done every single day. One of the things that I've done is make sure that I knock down at least three of those tasks so I can keep on moving forward. Whenever we're thinking about creating any kind of result in our life, we have to start at the end and work our way back. And then once you can make a goal look like a reality and create the tasks and the steps to get there, you're going to realize that every single thing that we want in life is attainable. Now, this leads me to number 28, which is the paradox, the antithesis to what I just said. So, when you set a goal, that becomes the destination. Think about it as creating the destination on a GPS. And I'll tell you right now, which is something that you realize probably by attaining some of the goals in your life where you don't really feel happy once you attain the goal. The happiness was the striving. The happiness was the pushes, the work that you were doing, the process. So, one of my favorite rappers, his name is Russ. He has a line that says, "I enjoy the climb. I don't care where the summit is." And this is what encapsulates this point. When you focus hard on each and every step, you end up making the journey way more fun and exciting. And then this allows you to play an infinite game where you're getting the most joy out of doing the process and doing the work as opposed to achieving attainment. Now 29 is achieving goal is not about gaining things. It's actually about becoming the person who attracts the goal as a result of who they are. A goal changes you. It levels you up. You literally cannot be the same person achieving a bigger goal than you were before that. You need new behaviors. You need new ways of thinking. So as you are doing this journey, as you're on this process, focus on the behaviors that it would take for you to attain this goal and then keep on repeating those over and over and over until they become who you are. The thing about chasing shortcuts is you take away the skills that you need in order to just keep the result that you are trying to attain in the first place. When it comes to getting things in life, you want to choose the long path because it is always going to be the shortest. Show up when you're not motivated. Show up when you're feeling it. Show up when you're not feeling it. Just this step alone puts you ahead of 80% of people who only show up when they feel like doing it. Step two, when you're there, do the work. It sounds easy, but there's a difference between deep work and shallow work. You want to remove all distractions and focus in on what you are doing. Put full attention on the work you need to do, and you will be rewarded with riches. And the last step, which is the most overlooked part, is to look for ways to improve. So, you can be showing up, you can be doing the work, but if you're showing up and doing the work on the wrong things, then you're not necessarily going to move forward. It is why I have adopted a mindset of canai, which is constant and neverending improvement. You can always look for ways to get better at anything you do. I've used this formula to get myself in shape, to build businesses, to grow on social media. It is simple, but don't take it for easy. And I swear, apply this part to your life and you will be way ahead of everyone else that doesn't. And here's the final one. People overestimate what they can do in 6 weeks, but they underestimate what they can do in a year. Imagine all those things that you just quit because it got a little bit too hard and you stuck with it for a full year. You would be way ahead of where you are and you'll probably be very surprised at what you accomplish. My biggest regrets in life come from not doing the thing when I should have done it. Something you may not know about me is that I started a YouTube channel 15 years ago and I was just posting sporadically and then it got to a point where I'm like, I'm not getting anything out of this. I might as well just quit. And I look back on that with regret because if I just kept at it for a full year, I'd probably be way past where I am at this very moment. I know hindsight's 2020, but still, while everyone is trying to give you these shortcuts and these short time horizons, get rich quick, get fit quick, whatever it is, adopt a mindset of I'll do it for as long as it takes. Want to get myself to a specific shape? As long as it takes. Want to build a business? As long as it takes. What this mindset does is it allows you to make mistakes. It allows you to not try to be perfect. It allows you not to go all or nothing. So instead of giving yourself 6 weeks or 6 months or whatever, give yourself a full year to really attract this level of success that you want in your life. I promise you this is one of the best ways to avoid regret. So when you're watching this, I want you to remember to not take my word on any of this. - 5 Women Entrepreneurs Share Their Secrets To Success | Refinery29: Kind: captions Language: en Inspiration can come from any direction. But how do you take that new concept and make it a reality? In advance of the Inc. Women’s Summit, I’m meeting up with some of my favorite entrepreneurs to talk about what empowers them on a daily basis. You have all created businesses from out-of-the-box thinking. What made you veer left when everybody else was going right? Bobbi. When I moved to New York, it was the ‘80s. And everyone was doing the over made up contour look. I know it’s back. I still don’t like contour. So I would make up the models to make them look like they weren’t wearing makeup. And that was my calling card. But there was no makeup available that allowed you to do that. I met a chemist one day at a shoot and I said I can’t find a lipstick that didn’t smell, wasn’t greasy, and I wanted to match lips. Then I realized, “Wow. This is something.” For me, entrepreneurship, it wasn’t something that I aspired to. I always wanted to be an inventor. If I can be the perfect balance between Beyoncé and Bill Nye the Science Guy every single day… Now we’re installing our technologies around different countries in emerging markets. Turning everything from floors and roads to baby strollers into energy-generating solutions. You guys both touched on this idea of identifying problems that existed in your life and looking at ways that you could shape an industry, rather than looking at the industry itself. I started a fashion technology company with my co-founder. We had no fashion or technology experience. We knew what it meant to buy a dress for $1,000, wear it once, and then be photographed on social media. And feel like you could never wear it again. So we were like, “Well, that doesn’t make sense.” Renting dresses made sense. If you thought too far down the road, it could be easy to find all the why not’s versus the how can you make this happen. I want to hear Angela’s story talking about how you are helping young entrepreneurs. So I started NewME, which is an accelerator for minorities in 2011. We focus only on the technology industry, which at the time, it was a young, technical, white guys’ club. I did not really start NewME as a business. It was a problem that I wanted to solve. And I thought it was going to be like a one-time project. What do you see is standing in between these women and the dollars that you know they need to succeed? When I work with men, they are not scared to ask for money even if what they’re working on is mediocre. But women can have something phenomenal but it’s like we hold ourselves back. So a lot of the work that I do in particular with women is confidence building, is getting them comfortable with asking for the money. Priya, I want to talk about the impact you are having on actual people: helping them create wealth in the way that they've never even thought of before. Historically, financial planning has been reserved for the wealthy. And when I was working on Wall Street, my clients were old and rich. And all of my friends at the same time were wondering where do we go for the answers. There’s this completely underserved kind of ignored demographic that we call Henrys, high earners not rich yet. I so often hear young women say, “I just want to be paid what I’m worth.” I don’t even know what that means. And I very rarely hear young women say, “I want to make a lot of money.” Right? It’s as if we all sort of internalize this idea that the budget’s not there. Research show that men are four times more likely than women to ask. So at Stash Wealth, salary negotiations come up. Set it as a goal though and it has to be a very concrete goal. Like I want to buy a $250,000 house in three years so I want to get a raise and let’s back into what you need to do today to be on track. The flipside that millennials have to be very careful of is entitlement. For example, one kid said, “I feel like I really need to make x.” I’m like, “Oh, that’s interesting. For this job, if you do all of this research, this is the highest you could get paid.” And he’s like, “Yeah, I didn’t really think about what the job was. I just kind of thought New York.” What are you saying to me? I don’t think women talk about comp and salaries in the same way men do. Women I advise, some of the most valuable help I give them is actually talking in a real way about what you should be making. But getting the data out there— that’s hard especially amongst women because they’re fewer women who are in leadership roles. I find younger women are much more transparent with their salary. For them it’s not TMI, it’s cocktail talk. And I think that that lifting the veil —is going to get us closer to equality. –I think that’s really important. In “The Big Life,” I have an entire chapter devoted to the side hustle secret to success. How do you know when it’s time to make your side hustle your main hustle? I fully believe in the side hustle and in fact, when I worked in finance, because I was not passionate about finance, I would spend whatever time I had doing college essay editing, which I kind of made all the business around. But ultimately went to business school and co-founded Rent the Runway in business school. So I was kind of side hustling it. I was taking classes but I’m a pretty risk averse entrepreneur so the whole way, I had a timeline of milestones we had to meet. And I think it was helpful for me and my co-founder, kept us honest. Ultimately one of those milestones was raising money. And for me, raising money was another way of checking myself on. Was this a good concept that VC’s would put their name behind? So I very much believe that parallel processing, kind of always having a little bit of a safety net, and just being honest with yourself if something’s not working. And forcing that brutal honesty, it’s hard. You need to be able to sell something or it’s not a business. Don’t quit your job. Don’t do anything until you figure that out. And then even after you figure that out, you got to do a deeper dive into the customer and try to find more customers like the ones who gave you a dollar. When I’m talking to young women, they will ask me, “How do you get ahead in a male-dominated industry if you’re a woman?” The advice I always give is to forget you’re a woman, right? You are a person with a good idea. Just say it. That was exactly the advice that I gave myself was just to remember that you’re there like any other employee and to speak up when you have a great idea. I did get called in once. My boss told me that my attitude with my colleagues was abrasive. And I wasn’t sure if it was because I was a female. I’ve been in rooms with investors who are supposed to be investing in women and people of color and have the investor say, “So who’s the tech lead on this?” I’m like, “It’s me.” “Well, who came up with this?” I’m like, “Ask me a question first and see if I don’t have the answer.” And then all of a sudden they’re just like, “So did your father help you…” “Did my father, what?” I actually disagree that you should forget that you’re a woman. Every single day I get up being like, “Jessica, you are a black woman. You’re going to have to do three times as much to get half as far.” Is anyone besides me been called difficult? Oh yeah. I love it though. Me too. It’s a compliment. When I was younger, I didn’t like it. Maybe I should not say stuff back as much. But the older I got, I’m like I kind of like that about me. We all know that we can’t just be like one of the boys that—but the good news is that we have our special perspective. You embrace the difference, right? I don’t think a man could have started Rent the Runway. There’s this old idea that women are in competition with each other. That there’s backstabbing. That there’s only room at the table for one woman. But I’ve been so inspired by how millennial women have replaced competition with collaboration. Being someone who has been in the industry for a long time, when I was first starting my company especially with other makeup artists, you would never give any advice. Never would they ever tell you —because everyone was a—right. —I was about to say. Everyone was in competition. And now as I’m reinventing myself and need some different things, they’re here. Call this person. Do this. This is where you get this. It’s an amazing change. It is. This year I turned 60 and I started a new lifestyle brand. There’s more and more opportunity. And there’s a million things you’re going to end up doing. On your own terms. Be the entrepreneur of your life.
