Healthy Eating 101: Kind: captions Language: ar مرحباّ أنا الدكتور مايك إيفانز واليوم سوف أتكلم عن الأكل الصحي وهو موضوع ضخم على الرغم من أنه يبدو سهلاَ ولكنه في الواقع معقد للغاية ذلك فقد رأيت أنه من الأفضل أن تركز على ما سأقوله ذا قررت أن تجلس معي في العيادة ما رأيك أن نبدأ بالتساؤل عن ما تريد تحقيقه خسارة الوزن؟ التقليل من الإفراط في الأكل؟ الأكل الصحي؟ حياة صحية أطول؟ إذاََ فما يهمك هو الصورة العامة وأود الإشارة إلى أن الأكل هو مجرد سلوك وحيد في السلسلة الصحية فالتمرين البدني المنتظم يؤدي إلى تحكم أفضل بالضغوطات النفسية ونوم أفضل وهو ما يقود إلى خيارات غذائية أفضل بالتالي إلى طاقة أكبر واحتمالية أقل للإصابة بأمراض مزمنة وهكذا أما إذا كنت تسعى أن تنقص وزنك؟ فنظرياَ ذلك سهل "لا تأكل كثيراَ، وتحرك أكثر من المعتاد " ولكن المشكلة تكمن في إبقاء ميزان السعادة متزناَ على أرض الواقع ففي الدول المتقدمة نحن محاطون بأطعمة رخيصة الثمن لا حصر لها ذات سعرات حرارية مرتفعة، ومذاقات متنوعة، وأحجام هائلة أما الطرف الآخر من معادلة ميزان الطاقة النشاط العالي، فلقد تغير أيضاَ حيث أن جيلنا الحالي يعاني كثيراَ من جراء داء الجلوس مشاهدة التلفاز ليلاَ، التنقل من وإلى العمل، وألعاب الفيديو المذهلة، والأرصفة المتحركة والكثير منا الآن يعمل في وظائف معرفية فثقافتنا تدفعنا نحو الأزرار السهلة بدلاَ من جعل يومنا صعباَ أما في الطرف الآخر الآخر من المعادلة أعتقد أنه من الضروري أن لا نغفل عن روعة الطعام فالطعام يجمع العوائل، ويبني العلاقات، ويمدنا بالصحة حسناَ فلنبدأ بأكثر سؤال يردني حول الأكل ما هي أفضل حمية لخسارة الوزن؟ أنا لا أستغرب حيرة الناس فبينما تشاهد هذا المقطع فعلى الأرجح أن هنالك نافذة منبثقة تتكلم عن صناعة الحمية مثل حمية الديتوكس أو نظام سوبر فود الغذائي ناهيك عن أن مشاهير هوليوود بدأوا باتباع نظام معين وليس هنالك بحث يدل على أن هنالك نظاما غذائيا تجارياَ يعد هو الأفضل والشيء الوحيد الذي من الممكن أن يضمن نجاح سير النظام هو مدى الإلتزام بكل تبعات النظام الذي تختاره ولكي نعيد صياغة ما أوردنا فإن مجتمعنا لا يزال يتمرجح ما بين حمية وحمية بحثاَ عن تركيبة سحرية ولكن السر لا يكمن في التركيبة إنما في النسق بدلا من اتباع نظام غذائي معين بحذافيره يخبرنا العلم أن نختار الحمية التي نحب أكثر من غيرها بحيث أننا سنتقيدبها فعلاَ في 2015 كانت أبحاث مؤسسة كوكرين الطبية تبحث في لعوامل التي من شأنها أن تتسبب في نجاح الحميات التجارية ولم تكن التقليل من الكربوهيدرات أو الدهون لكن كانت في التنظيم والدعم المعنوي الشخصي الحميات الغذائية هي مجرد قواعد في الأكل ولا تؤثر على نسقنا الغذائي و كما يدعوها الاقتصاديون جهاز الالتزام مع إدراك الناس أن ما يفعلونه تحسين قدرتهم في التحكم بمستقبلهم غير منطقي ويعد اندفاعاَ فحسب فبدلاَ من البقاء على وضع الطيار الآلي فإن الحمية تنبهك من خلال النسق الغذائي بإتجاه خيارات محددة في الأكل وفي الغالب تقلل من الافراط في الأكل كل الأنظمة الغذائية التجارية تمتلك تركيبتها السحرية وهو ما نسميه بالمغذيات(ماكرونيوترينتس) فمثلاََ كاربوهيدرات أقل، وبروتين أكثر، ودهون وسكر أقل وهكذا وعادة ما تكون لديهم أسباب لتعليل طريقتهم مثلاَ يمكنك أن تأكل مثل كرجل الكهف أو استخدام نظام التسجيل أو المعلبات أو حتى متابعة أطباء مشهورين في هذا المجال أو حتى غير ذلك و الرسالتان اللتان أود توجيههما بشأن المغذيات هما واحد...أظن بأننا نبذل الكثير من الوقت والجهد,ونحن نركز عليها واثنان...تظل المسألة مسألة نوعية وليست كمية كاربوهيدرات أقل...حسناَ الكاربوهيدرات يمكن أن تكون صحية في صيغها المعقدة فواكه، وخضروات، وبقوليات، والحبوب الكاملة وليست صحية جداَ في صيغها البسيطة مثل السكر المصفى والنشا المعالج ولكن يجب نكون منصفين لكاربوهيدرات لها مذاق هائل ومجتمعنا عادة ما يميل إلى تناولها بكثرة لذلك فإن الناس الذي يقللون مقدار ما يتناولونه يومياَ تميل أجسامهم بأن تخسر بعض الوزن إلا أنه عندما ندرس نتائج فقدان الوزن النسبية في بحث منهجي في عام 2014 للدكتور سيليست ناود وزملائه لاحظوا فيه العلاقة ما بين الوزن ومؤشرات القلب والأوعية الدموية على الأناس الذين يتبعون الحمية قليلة الكاربوهيدرات ولقد استمرت لمدة سنتين وكانت المحصلة أنه ليس هنالك فرق مقارنة مع حمية تقليل الوزن المعتدلة ولكن ماذا عن حمية تقليل السكريات حسناََ إذا كنت سأصف كلمة السكر بكلمة واحدة فقط فستكون "مخادع" فالكثير والكثير من السكريات قد وجدت طريقها إلى أنظمتنا الغذائية اليومية حيث أن الكثير من المشروبات تحتوي على ما لا يقل عن 8 ملاعق من السكر فمتوسط ما يتناوله الفرد الأمريكي من السكر هو 20 ملعقة شاي وأغلب هولاء الأشخاص هم المراهقون وأقل في كندا وهذه الأطعمة حلوة فعلاَ ولكن الوضع يتشابه مع الأطعمة التي نظنها صحية مثل حبوب الإفطار وألواح الجرانولا (الشوفان المحلى) وعصائر الفواكه عندما تساهم الزيادة في السكر في ارتفاع السعرات الحراية فهنا يمكن أن نقول بأن هنالك مشكلة وهو إنذار حقيقي فعندما أفحص مريضا َتظهر لديه مقدمات وأعراض مرض السكري أول ما يقومون به هو إسقاط السكر من نظامهم الغذائي إلا أنه إذا نظرنا لتجارب الوقاية من السكري فإن القليل منها تشير إلى أهمية التقليل من السكر بينما الأغلب تشدد على أهمية النسق الصحي وأن يكون الفرد نشيطاَ لنصف ساعة يومياَ لنقل أن 5-7% من الوزن يقل عند التقليل من تناول الدهون المشبعة وزيادة الطعام الذي يحتوي على الألياف وهو ما يقلل من نسبة الإصابة بالسكري بنسبة %58 ماذا عن الأطعمة قليلة الدسم؟ حسنا... أظن بأن القصة المأثورة قد تغيرت عن الدهون من أن كل الدهون مضرة إلى أن الموضوع يتعلق بالاستمرارية فلديك أمثلة من الأطعمة على الدهون الغير مشبعة الوجبات السريعة المقلية والكثير من الأغذية المعلبة ولكن ليست مفيدة ويجب أن نقلل من استهلاكنا لها ثم يوجد لدينا الدهون المشبعة وغالباَ ما تكون في الألبان واللحوم الحمراء والنباتات الزيتية مثل جوز الهند أو النخيل ويمكن القول بأنها ليست جيدة عند الإكثار منها ولكن جيدة عند الاعتدال فيها ثم هنالك الدهون الأحادية الغير مشبعة أو (موفا) حمية البحر المتوسط والتي سأشرحها بعد ثوان تعتمد بشكل كبير على الموفا فالأفوكادو، وحبوب المكسرات، وزيت الزيتون، الشوكولاتة الداكنة وهو ما يعطي فوائد صحية وأخيراَ لدينا (بوفا)، أو الدهون المتعددة الغير مشبعة وهي تعد أكبر جزء في سلسلة الدهون وتوجد في الأسماك الغنية بالزيوت والتجارب الماضية تظهر بأن هنالك تراجعاَ في المؤشرات المتعلقة بالقلب بينما التجارب الأحدث ليست متفائلة كثيراَ حيث أن التحاليل تشير أنه لاتزال لا توجد مضار وهناك فوائد بسيطة لذلك فإن ما اقترحه هو تناول وجبتين في الأسبوع من البوفا يبدو بأن الناس يؤدون أفضل عندما يستبدلون الدهون المشبعة بدهون الموفا و البوفا حسناَ ماذا عن الحميات ذات البروتين العالي مرة أخرى...المسألة تعتمد على النوعية أكثر من الكمية فالبروتين يأتي في علب مختلفة بتأثيرات مختلفة على الصحة فمقارنة لحم كثير الملح مع شريحة سمك سلمون أو العدس أو حتى حفنة من اللوز أكثر المعلومات تشير أنه إذا تناولت البروتين الصحي اللحوم البيضاء وأنواع المكسرات والأسماك فسيكون عطاؤك أكثر ولاسيما إذا ما توزعت على اليوم كامل وخصوصا في وجبة الإفطار هنالك أيضا بعض الحميات التي تكون نتائجها جيدة على الناس الذين يعانون من أمراض فعلي سبيل المثال حمية الداش تخفض ما بين 5-11ملم من الزئبق أو انخفاض نسبة السكر في الدم في مؤشر النظام الغذائي يخفض (آي ون سي) وهو مقياس السكر في الدم مع مرور الوقت في الأشخاص الذين يعانون من مرض السكري بنسبة 0.5% الكثير من المرضى يعانون من ارتفاع في نسبة الكولسترول وقد قام مؤخراَ د.ديفيد جنكينس وزملائه في جامعة تورنتو بإظهار أنه يمكنهم خفض نسبة الكولسترول بنسبة 35% باتباعهم لحمية البورتفوليو (قليلة الدهون المشبعة وكثيرة الألياف) وأغلب المعلومات عن النظرية النباتية جاءت من دراسات الأتراب أو ما تسمى بالتعرض أيضاَ والآن هنالك جلسات عشوائية تشير إلى أن يؤدون أفضل بها من الصعب أن لا تستنتج أن الحمية التي ترتكز على النباتات والطعام الغير معالج هي حمية ذكية وبالطبع فإن الكثير من الناس يقومون باختيار مميز وهي أن العبء يقل على كاهل كوكبنا كل ما زاد عدد الأشخاص النباتيين هنالك طريقة للتفكير بكل هذا وهي بالتعقيب على حمية البرازيل التوجيهية الغذائية وفيها قد حولوا توجههم من تمام التنوع في المغذيات إلى نظام أكثر تقبلا وإذا نظرنا للموضوع عن بعد نحن نفضل شراء الأطعمة المعالجة والمعلبة التي يمكن أكلها في أي مكان ويمكن أن يكون الطعام صحياَ أكثر ويقوي العلاقات بين الأفراد عن طريق إعداد الوجبات بين أفراد العائلة والأصدقاء حسناَ، إذا ليس هنالك تركيبة سحرية أو حمية مطلقة الفعالية؟ فالإجابة هي نعم لأنني أرى بأن الحمية متعلقة بالثقافة أكثر من غيرها وكذلك العادات البسيطة فالحمية لا تركز على خسارة الوزن ولكنها تركز على المحصلات الصحية مثل التقليل من ظهور مرض السرطان وأمراض القلب، والأمراض العقلية، وزيادة العمر المتوقع و استناداَ للأدلة فإن أفضل حمية هي حمية البحر المتوسط فبدلاَ من اتباع قواعد في الأكل ،والالتزام بثوابت في الغذاء الأمر يتعلق أكثر بالاعتدال في تناول لحوم أقل، وخضراوات أكثر، وفواكه في التحلية وهذا النوع معني أكثر بأقسام الخضراوات والفواكه في المحلات أو البقالات التي تبيع منتجات جزر البحر المتوسط يطلق عليها اسم حمية البحر المتوسط ولكنها في الحقيقة أسلوب حياة ففي تلك المنطقة يكون الأفراد كثيري النشاط، وتكون وجباتهم معتدلة ويحظون بدعم اجتماعي جيد فيما بينهم إذاَ فلنكمل حديثنا عن التحول من الحميات الغذائية إلى السلوكيات الصحية وذلك بالنظر إلى المركز الوطني (إن دبليو سي أر) للتحكم بالوزن التي تؤثر على طريقة أكلنا حيث يقوم المركز بتوزيع استبيان سنوي على أكثر من 10000 شخص بحيث يكون عدد النساء أكثر من الرجال للأشخاص الذين خسروا بعض الوزن وحافظوا على ذلك لما يزيد عن سنة ومن غير المفاجئ أن 98% قد أجروا بعض التغييرات على ما يتناولون بينما قام 94% بزيادة النشاط والحركة في جداولهم ولكن لم تكن هنالك تركيبة واحدة فلقد قام الأغلب بحصر الأكل الذي يتناولونه بحساب السعرات الحرارية أما البقية فقد استمروا في أكلهم المعتاد ولكن قللوا الكميات فحسب 70%منهم يتناولون وجبة الإفطار الغالبية يشاهدون التلفاز لأقل من 10 ساعات ويأكلون ثلاث مرات فقط من خارج المنزل أسبوعياَ وبشكل عام فإن هولاء الناس يمارسون الأنشطة البدنية لأكثر من نصف ساعة يومياَ لأكثر من نصف ساعة يومياَ ويبلغ متوسط ما يمارسونه ساعةَ تقريباَ وفي الغالب تكون مشياَ وتقريباَ أجمع جميع من شارك في الاستبانة بأن خسارة الوزن ترفع مستويات الطاقة، وحركة الجسد،وتحسن المزاج وتزيد الثقة بالنفس، والصحة الجسدية الملاحظة الدورية خطوة بالغة في الأهمية خسارة الوزن أمر مهم ولكن الجزء الصعب هو في المحافظة على الوزن الجديد لما لا يقل عن 80% من أشخاص بيننا زن نفسك واستخدم هذه الطريقة كمنبه لك لخياراتك في تناول طعامك اليومي ومثال على ذلك أن 75% من الأشخاص الذين كانوا عينة في دراسة مركز التحكم الوزن يقيسون وزنهم أسبوعياَ بينما 36% منهم بشكل يومي وينظرون لمسألة الوقوف على الميزان على أنها محاكمة لإيقاف زيادة الوزن حيث أن الوقوف على الميزان يومياَ يخفض من احتمالية زيادة في الوزن بما متوسطه2.3 كغ أو 5 أرطال كذلك من الأمثلة على الملاحظات المنبّهة مذكرة الطعام حتى ولو كانت لأسبوع فقط وقد أصبحت أسهل اليوم مع وجود التطبيقات عادة يتجاهل المرضى التدوين فيها لأنهم يظنون الموضوع بسيط ولكن مشاهدة ما الذي أكلته قد يضاعف من خسارتك للوزن ولهذا فأنا أعتقد أن الوعي بهذه الأمور في متناول اليد فقد يكون ما تتناوله وقد يكون بقياسك لوزنك ولكن يجب علينا معرفة أن الحياة فوضوية أيضاَ ولكي نستمتع بها فإننا نحتاج للمرونة فببساطة أنت تشعر بأنك جيد إذا أكلت جيداَ ولهذا فأنا أحبذ فكرة البدء بتغييرات بسيطة أو ما أسميه بالقرصات أو ربما تغيير شي تفعله كثيرا فعلى سبيل المثال تناول الإفطار أو الوجبات الخفيفة أو المشروبات يمكنك مثلاَ أن تستبدل حبوب فطورك المعتادة ببعض دقيق الشوفان أو رقائق القمح أو حفنة من اللوز بدلاَ من كيس رقائق البطاطس أو ربما شرب الماء بدل ما تشربه من مشروبات ملوّنة أعلم أن هذا لا يبدو مغرياَ ولكن د.مايك أخصائي تغذية وقد يتمكن من شطب رطل من وزنك أسبوعياَ أو ربما أكثر كما أنه قد يجعلك تشعر بتحسن ومن منظور آخر، الإضافة بدلاَ من النقصان لذلك فإن د.شيري باجوتو وزملاؤها قاموا باختيار عينة عشوائية من الأفراد المعرضين للإصابة بمتلازمة الأيض ووزعوا إما على جمعية القلب الأمريكي للحمية أو في المقابل باتباع نصيحة سهلة وهي زيادة الألياف يومياَ بمقدار 30 جرام فمثلاَ امتطاء عربة نخالة القمح ثم زيارة مدينة الفوليات وأخذ بعض التوت كلتا المجموعتين خسرت وزناَ إلا أن مجموعة الجمعية خسرت وزناَ أكثر بفارق بسيط وكذلك فإن نتائج كلا المجموعتين انعكست بشكل إيجابي على وظائف الجهاز الدوري حسناَ يمكن القول أنني أحبذ فكرة إدراج الطعام الصحي بدلاَ من تطويق الأطعمة الأخرى أو إقصائها كما أنه هناك مصادر تشير أن الشعور بالتخمة أو الإحساس بالإمتلاء لا ينحصر فقط على السعرات الجرارية مكتسبة فالسعرات الحرارية المكتسبة من البروتينات والألياف تجعلنا نشعر أكثر امتلاءَ ومن زاوية أخرى فإن الدعم الاجتماعي بمقدوره أن يساعدنا في عادات أكلنا في دراسة أخرى لتقويم العادات الغذائية إما بمفردهم أو مع 3 أصدقاء فأكثر أو مع أفراد العائلة لدعمهم في تقويم العادات في 10 أشهر وفي المحصلة فإن النسبة كانت 24% للأفراد ثم أصبحت 66%بعد الحصول على دعم الأهل والأصدقاء وفي موضوع الدعم فإن تواصلك مع أخصائي تغذية حتى ولو كان عن طريق الإنترنت من شأنه أن يساعد لأنهم يمتلكون ما يعرف بالجينيس بار لأكلك والحيوية هي الأخرى نقطة مهمة في سياق الأكل والتغذية حيث تشير - 10 Simple Habits for a More Balanced Lifestyle: Kind: captions Language: en Ten simple habits to lose weight naturally. How many times year did you start a new diet? Does it really work? Weight loss doesn't have to be stressful or complicated. All you have to do is build simple habits to see the weight come off. Here are ten scientifically proven tips on how to achieve your dream body in almost no time. Number ten - use blue plates. One recent study has shown that the color blue decreases appetite. So try some changes like decorating your table with a blue tablecloth, blue napkins, blue plates, and glasses. This trick is called psychology of colors, and some colors actually stimulate your appetite. Blue will do the opposite for you. Number nine - be more optimistic. Every positive thought matters. The idea is to focus only on good thoughts and repeat your goals as if they were your mantra. For example, 'I lost half a pound! That's just great!' 'Slowly, but surely!' 'I can eat only the portion I took.' 'I can say NO to dessert.' Such optimistic phrases will help you achieve your goals very fast. Number eight - thirty minutes of exercise is enough. A healthy diet plan does help you lose weight, but it's critical to combine good nutrition with some physical exercise. Even lower risk activities, such as walking will be beneficial. Good news! Researchers at the University of Copenhagen, Denmark, have found that exercising for 30 minutes per day helps you lose 25% more weight than going for a full hour. Number seven - eat slowly and eat alone. In this fast-paced world, fast eating has become a bad habit. Ironically enough, it reduces the feeling of satiety and leads to overeating. When your brain registers that your stomach is full, you've already eaten much more than your body needs. When you're eating out, always choose the smallest portion. Take your time to read the menu. Even if it's a place where they sell hamburgers and other unhealthy food, choose the smallest portion: mini burger, junior popcorn, personal pizza, or light salad. According to recent studies, people who don't order their main dish can see everything on the table even if they're already full. Number six - avoid eating in front of your computer or TV. According to the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, food ads, especially junk food ads, increase our appetite and make us overeat. Also, when we eat in front of our computers or TV, we don't pay attention to our food. Therefore we are not aware of how much we've eaten. Instead, you can try eating in front of a mirror. You may say it's just ridiculous, but scientific facts assure that eating while looking at yourself in the mirror reduces the amount of food you eat to one-third. Also, did you know that eating alone is better than eating with a group of friends? It's proven that people who eat in the group eat more. You spend more time at the table, and you can't control how much you've eaten while talking. It doesn't mean you should eat alone all the time, but try to bring to the table only if you're going to eat, only your portion. Number five - don't overheat your house. According to research published in the journal Obesity Reviews, there's a link between reduced exposure to seasonal cold and increases in obesity in the UK, US, and other developed countries. When we're exposed to cold, our skeletal muscles contract to generate heat, burning extra calories in the process We also produce heat even in mildly cold conditions when we're not shivering. So when the whole house is heated, we no longer have to adjust different temperatures, and we burn less energy. Number four - reading food labels. The Nutrition Facts food labels can be very useful in helping you make healthy choices and lose weight. A study published in the Journal of Consumer Affairs suggests that people who read food labels on a regular basis are more likely to lose weight, especially women between the ages of 37 and 50. Number three - eat more yogurt and nuts. According to research conducted at the Harvard School of Public Health, eating yogurt and nuts is more effective in the long run for weight loss than eating fruits and vegetables. Also, Louisiana State University research shows that people to eat nuts every day are 25% less likely to be obese and 21% less likely have a wider waistline. You're probably thinking 'That's crazy! All kinds of nuts are full of fat and calories!' It's not correct. 'Nuts are rich and plant protein, dietary fiber, and healthy mono- and polyunsaturated fatty acids,' explains Carol O'Neil, lead researcher and professor at Louisiana State University. 'Therefore, nuts are a satiating food that may help suppress appetite.' In addition to all the benefits, nuts will help you feel satiated: you'll eat less and lose weight more effectively. Number two - add hot peppers and spices in your diet. Consuming chilies and other hot peppers also helps to make the pounds fly off since they accelerate your metabolism. Also, cayenne pepper reduces appetite and burns calories faster, according to research from Purdue University. Number one - drink plenty of water. You should stay well hydrated every day. Make it a habit of drinking a glass of water in the morning on an empty stomach and a glass of water before going to sleep. This trick keeps your body hydrated, generates satiety, and speeds up your metabolism, all of which helps you lose weight. A clinical trial presented at a meeting of the American Chemical Society confirmed that drinking two glasses of water before eating helps a person to lose weight and fight obesity. Here's a bonus. A simple trick that will help you control your weight. If you're hungry, smell bananas. This may sound a little crazy, but smelling foods like apples, mint, or banana can fool your brain and make it believe that you're eating. A study involving 3,000 volunteers who smelled these foods when they were hungry found that their appetites decreased. And the results were soon reflected on the scales. Amazing, huh? So, what helps you stay in shape and control your weight? Share your thoughts in the comments below! Don't forget to hit the like button and click subscribe to stay on the bright side of life.

- Doctor explains INTERMITTENT FASTING for weight loss + METHODS and 10 FOODS TO EAT AND AVOID!: Kind: captions Language: en foreign we're going to discuss the role of intermittent fasting to facilitate weight loss including the science behind intermittent fasting how it works in practice side effects and tips for what you could eat and also foods to try and avoid now before we get into the main section of the video I do want to stress that I've designed this as a general educational resource it's not a clinical advice video and I'd strongly suggest that if you do have a medical condition such as diabetes and you're thinking about making changes to your diet you should first discuss this with your healthcare provider especially if you're taking medication so let's start off the video by discussing what intermittent fasting is well put quite simply intermittent fasting involves cycling between periods of fasting and eating and there are two main ways of doing this in practice and we'll discuss these in more detail later in the video These are essentially called the 5 2 or 16 8 diets we'll discuss the specific science behind intermittent fasting in the next section of the video but in a nutshell when only fast to use up stored energy by burning excess body fat and this results in weight loss so now that we have a basic understanding of what intermittent fasting is why don't we briefly look at the science behind intermittent fasting because I do think that it's worth trying to understand what the theory behind intermittent fasting is to try help us make sense of how this particular form of dieting does help us to lose weight so when we we take in more energy from food then can immediately be used by the body a hormone called insulin is produced when we eat which breaks down carbohydrates into glucose glucose can then be used by the cells for energy or it can be stored in the liver or muscle as glycogen when we need a quick source of energy or the body's not getting glucose from food the glycogen is released from the liver and broken down into glucose and sent into the bloodstream however there's limited storage space for glycogen and once stores are full the liver will turn excess glucose into fat now some of this fat will be stored in the liver or it will be exported to other fat deposits in the body now when you fast the insulin levels fall this signals the body to start burning stored energy and one of these stored energy sources that the body can use is glycogen in the liver and the body will use that first as it's most accessible after the glycogen has been used up the body will start to break down fat to get the energy that it needs if we start to burn the stores of energy such as the glycogen or fat then in theory we should lose weight so now we know a little bit more about the science behind intermittent fasting well what are the different methods for fasting well the two most well-known methods for intermittent fasting are the 5-2 diet this is where you normally eat for five days a week then eat only around 500 to 600 calories on the other two days the second option is time restricted eating where you have a long period in the day when you don't eat with the time restricted eating most people choose something called a 16 8 cycle this involves not eating for 16 hours of the day then having an eight hour eating window and generally this diet form is done daily or almost daily intermittent fasting can help you lose body fat as it works out to approximately a 25 total calorie deficit for the week however you do need to be mindful of the foods that you're eating in the FED state if you overeat or you're eating high calorie foods then you won't see the weight reduction that you're looking for so now we know a little bit more about the two main practical methods for intermittent fasting why don't we look at five foods that you might want to consider incorporating into your diet and also five foods to try and avoid so for the days and times that you're not fasting you're going to want to try and keep your diet in line with General Health eating guidelines and to help you with this I'm going to highlight some key food groups that you might want to consider so the first are whole grains these come onto your starchy carbohydrates and these will provide slow release of glucose into the bloodstream they're also an important source of fiber vitamins and minerals and examples of these are are oats whole grain bread whole grain pasta as well as brown rice now the second food group are lean meats and fish and these are important because they're packed full of protein and this is essential for the growth and repair of muscles and tissues the best kinds are lean meats such as skinless chicken and low-fat turkey white fish such as haddock or Cod are lean sources of protein and oily fish like salmon sardines tuna mackerel they're full of omega-3 fatty acids which are important for brain and heart health now the third food group to consider are dairy products which can be a rich source of calcium which is really important for bone health but you should try to choose low-fat varieties of dairy products things like skimmed milk and low-fat Greek yogurt fourth group are obviously fruits and vegetables these provide us with an important source of vitamins minerals as well as fiber and finally lentils beans and pulses are also good because they're low in fat and they're an important source of protein fiber as well as vitamins and minerals now those are the five food groups that I think you should try and incorporate into your diet but some people will ask well what food should I avoid well on an intermittent fasting diet foods that you should avoid are things like refined carbohydrates this means carbohydrates that have been highly processed and they contain little of the original grain and this is important because these foods are going to cause a rapid spike in your blood glucose levels and these are things like white flour white bread white rice white pasta many breakfast cereals they're usually packed full of unrefined sugars the next one is obviously sugar and this includes simple sugars like table sugar maple syrup but also foods that contain added sugar things like sugary drinks sweets chocolates ice creams and biscuits now the other food group to try and avoid are calm complex animal fats these are things like dripping and lard and these should be avoided on a low fat diet because they're high in saturated fat you should also try avoid too much red or processed meat so Meats like bacon and sausages these contain lots of saturated fats and try to stick to lean meats or try to cut any visible fats off if you're eating meat finally processed foods these are foods that have been altered in some way during preparation things like cakes pastries and biscuits as well as pies and convenience Foods they tend to be high in calories due to the amount of saturated fat as well as added sugar so what are the possible side effects of an intermittent fasting diet well some people might experience headaches and constipation but the risk of this can be minimized by making sure you're drinking plenty of fluids on the fasting days as well as eating plenty of vegetables and fruits again I do want to stress that if you're considering the intermittent fasting style of dieting then please consider this in the risk of your own personal health and medical conditions and if if you've got any questions or concerns before starting this diet then please do try to speak to a trained and registered dietitian or your doctor who will be able to advise you on the sustainability as well as suitability of this in relation to your own personal medical history so that brings us to the end of this video I hope you enjoyed it as well as learned something new and if you did please remember to consider liking the video leaving me a comment if you've got any other thoughts or you want to share your experiences with intermittent fasting both positive and negative and subscribe to the channel for weekly medical education videos if you've not done so already please also check out the references and resources that I've used to make this video These are going to be found in the description box and there's lots more useful information contained within these links finally I've got to stress this has been designed as an educational video it's not a clinical advice video the legal reasons please do read the full disclaimer in the description box as ever thank you for watching and until next time bye [Music]

  3. @Kevin-pi7rb: Google Reports:
Melissa McCarthy and Valerie Bertinelli have been frequently targeted by fraudulent online advertising, often featuring fake "keto" or "metabolism" gummies and supplements on YouTube and Facebook. These ads falsely use their names and likenesses to suggest they used these supplements for dramatic weight loss, which is not true. 
The Scam Tactics
Fake Endorsements: The ads often use manipulated images or fake news articles to claim celebrities lost weight using specific gummy brands.
"Keto Gummies" Scams: These ads typically promote "Keto One Gummies" or similar products, which are often scams with fake customer testimonials.
Fake News Websites: Many ads lead to websites designed to look like legitimate news sites or gossip blogs. 
Real Weight Loss Stories
Melissa McCarthy: McCarthy has addressed her weight loss as being achieved through conventional lifestyle changes, such as improved diet, consistent routine, and mindful living, rather than pills or extreme, quick-fix diets. She has previously mentioned trying a doctor-supervised, all-liquid plan for a role, but her recent, sustained weight loss is due to lifestyle adjustments.
Valerie Bertinelli: Bertinelli has been transparent about her weight loss journey, focusing on a healthy relationship with food and shifting her perspective on eating, rather than using popular, trendy supplements. She has publicly stated she did not use Ozempic or other GLP-1 drugs. 
Conclusion
These YouTube and social media ads are deceptive. The products are likely not endorsed by the celebrities mentioned, and no reputable celebrity endorses the "magic pill" approach often advertised.

- I Stopped Eating Carbs for 4 Weeks over 50 ... Here's What Happened | Christian Keto Weight Loss: Kind: captions Language: en Recently, I came across this video by nurse Levie, and she talked about all of the benefits that she got when she started cutting carbs for six weeks. And it seemed like a lot of people were really interested in what she had to say. So, I thought I would share my experience of my first four weeks of cutting carbs because I had some pretty dramatic results. Really, it was that first four weeks of cutting carbs that transformed me to where I am today. So, if you're new here, my name is Suzanne and I've been on a weight loss journey for about a year and a half now. Up to this date, I've lost about 30 lbs. I am over 50 years old. I'm kind of getting close to being in menopause. So, losing weight for me has been very challenging to say the least. So, a little bit about my backstory. Probably around 45. Up until then, I really didn't have a problem much with maintaining my weight around the 140 mark, which was never really my goal, but I was at least at a healthy weight, and it was a very comfortable weight for me. But around 45, uh, between adding some stress to my life with trying to take a really hard exam for a couple years and going into premenopause, I just found within a couple years, I had gained around 30 to 40 lbs. And no matter what I did, I just struggled to lose any weight. Now, I didn't realize at the time that it could be tied into menopause. I didn't realize that as I was getting older, it was actually getting harder for me to lose weight. And even though I knew it was harder for me to lose weight, I couldn't figure out why. So, about a year and a half ago almost in December of 2023, I watched a video by Zoe Science where they promised using this hack that you would be able to lose weight and all you'd have to do was just change this one thing. And that one thing was to eat your vegetable at the beginning of every single meal, including breakfast. And so I started using that hack and I started noticing that I was losing some weight pretty easily, at least easier than it was before. And that video was the start of me watching a lot of different videos about blood sugar and how eating carbohydrates were causing these blood sugar spikes. And these blood sugar spikes were causing me to basically not be able to eat at a deficit and they were making it difficult for my body to shed weight. So kind of over that first four weeks was really when I transitioned into slowly cutting down carbs down to the point where I was actually eating less than 20 gram of carbs per day, which is actually what I still do to this day. So, as I was going along this journey during that four weeks of cutting carbs, I noticed that there were a few benefits that were really helpful in me losing weight. And the first one was that for the first time, I actually had a little more energy during the day. So, prior to doing this, it was getting so bad that I was starting to feel like I needed a nap twice a day. I would feel like I needed a nap around midm morning and then I would take a nap and then in the afternoon I would feel like I needed a nap again. And this was the first time in my life that I've ever felt like I needed to take two naps. So when I started cutting out the carbs, I noticed all of a sudden I didn't feel like I needed naps during the day. Now, occasionally I would still feel like I would want to take one nap, but I completely stopped feeling like I needed to take multiple naps. I just had a little bit more energy in the morning. It wasn't like a huge energy burst like when you have one of those rockstar drinks. Wasn't that kind of energy. It was more of a lack of tiredness, a lack of needing to get back in bed and take a nap. And on top of that, I noticed during the night I was getting a little bit better sleep because one thing that I've noticed over the past year and a half as well, is that when I go back to eating a lot of carbohydrates, like say on a holiday or if I have a treat meal, which I do, I will notice that at about midnight or 2:00 in the morning, I will wake up and feel very hot. I'll be having hot flashes, sweating. But when I am eating very low carb, I don't have that at all. It completely goes away. And those hot flashes were waking me up at night because I would feel really hot and then I would wake up and then it would be really hard for me to fall back asleep because I was just so uncomfortable. Now, one of the third benefits that I found, and this is kind of a a strange benefit maybe to some of you, but it was still a benefit nonetheless, and that is I used to get a lot of gas. And I actually used to think it was because I was eating eggs. But what I realized when I started eating low carb was I think it was actually from eating the high-fiber, high carb diet. because when I completely eliminated most of the carbohydrates, my gas almost went away entirely. And it wasn't until I did that that I realized it was actually the carbs that were the culprit and not the protein and the fat. Now, one of the bigger benefits that I really appreciated because weight loss has always been my goal was that I just wasn't as hungry. And being less hungry had a couple of additional benefits. First of all, because I was less hungry, I could actually lower the calories that I eat down to a deficit low enough to where I could lose weight, but I didn't feel hungry. So, the problem with trying to diet on a higher carb diet, or at least in my experience, I was eating 15 to,700 calories a day, high carb, but I still couldn't even get down to that because I would get hungry so often from the blood sugar spikes and drops that it was impossible for me to even hit that high of a target. And I say that high of a target because now I can eat between 1,00 and 1,200 calories on my low carb diet. And I never feel ravenous or feel like I have to just get to that next meal. I usually feel pretty satisfied. I don't feel like it takes a lot of effort for me to stay down at that calorie level. And that calorie level has been what has helped me to lose those 30 lbs over the last year and a half. So, of course, the final benefit that I've had, and I'm sure I've had more than this, but it's what I can remember right now, is the weight loss. So, pretty miraculously over that first four weeks of transitioning down to almost no carbs, I was able to lose 10 lbs in a month. I didn't feel hungry. I felt very satisfied. I actually felt a lot more in control because I wasn't getting those blood sugar drops making me feel like I had to eat right now. I wasn't getting cravings for the sweets that I'd been eating before. And it was just overall easier for me to be able to eat within the plan that I had crafted, stick to it, and lose weight. So, if you're curious exactly what plan I followed to lose that 10 pounds in the first four weeks, I did do a video where I went through and explained it in way more detail exactly what I did. The full name of the keto diet is the ketogenic diet. That means it generates ketones. And what are ketones? Ketones are an alternate fuel source, so it is a ketone producing diet. What do I mean by "and also a fuel source"? Well, our bodies can really survive on two fuel sources:&nbsp;sugar or fat. Sugar goes by different names: glucose, glycogen and of course sugar. So the&nbsp;theory goes that if we remove one fuel source, if we remove sugar, we're only left with one fuel source to burn - that's fat. So that's the most common appeal of a keto diet. But, how do you know&nbsp; you're in the keto diet? Well, you enter a state called ketosis and there are different ways of measuring ketosis including blood, breath and urine. The most accurate way is blood. To give you some&nbsp;references, the blood ketone levels of somebody on a normal diet, not on a keto diet, is less than&nbsp;0.1 mm/L. The Ketone levels of somebody who's on a keto diet is over 0.2&nbsp;mm/L. So, you don't really know if you're a ketogenic diet until you actually&nbsp; get measured. Maybe you're eating foods that you think are keto but if you're not measuring you're&nbsp;not really sure. Now, what does that look like from a diet composition? A regular mixed diet might be some between 40% and 65% carbohydrates, between 15% and 30% protein, and the remain will be fat. On a&nbsp;keto diet, it's about 60%+ fat, very low carbohydrates, typically 50 grams per day or lower, and remainder is protein, which is different than most other low carbohydrate diets. Most other low&nbsp;carbohydrate diets are "low carb - high protein". The keto diet is "low carb - moderate protein". So it's&nbsp;not as high in protein as a regular low carb diet and therefore keto levels on a low carb diet&nbsp;that's not ketogenic will be lower. So that is, these are the basics of the keto diet. Estrogen is largely made of fat, there is a mother molecule and that's&nbsp; cholesterol. Cholesterol is a fat molecule. During menopause estrogen levels sharply drop. So it's conceivable that a high fat diet will also increase estrogen levels which may negate&nbsp;menopausal symptoms. Things like hot flashes, poor sleep quality, vaginal dryness and others. On the other hand, it could also conceitedly cause issues because it is&nbsp; a low carbohydrate diet therefore it could cause some hypoglycemia or low blood sugar symptoms. They are plant estrogens. In other words they&nbsp;have similar effects to human estrogen, but are much weaker, but they do mitigate some of the&nbsp;symptoms of menopause. So,&nbsp;how do you know if you're adequately hydrated? One very simple test for that&nbsp; is what I call "the pee-test". That is if your urine is just lightly yellow, you're good. You have the right&nbsp; level of hydration. If your urine is deeply yellow, you're dehydrated. If your urine is completely&nbsp; clear, you're overhydrated. And that's how you can easily gauge your hydration status. Granted, the&nbsp;color of your urine may be affected by certain supplements or by certain medications, but in the&nbsp;absence of that, you can use the color of your urine as a hydration marker. Or very&nbsp;simply for the vast majority of people, their thirst signals are pretty good. If you're thirsty - drink.&nbsp;If you're not thirsty, don't bother. Simple as that. On a -10 to a +10 scale. So -10 to 0 means - I'm hungry. 0 to +10 means - I'm full. So imagine this: you're&nbsp;sitting down to eat and you're asking yourself how hungry or full am I? If you give yourself a&nbsp;rating of +3, that means you're not hungry. Then why are you eating? It's for reasons other&nbsp;than hunger and if that's the case, write down what emotion you're feeling in that moment. So how should you exercise&nbsp; if you're women over 50? It's been commonly said that when it comes to weight loss, nutrition plays&nbsp; 80% of the role and then exercise plays 20% of the role. That's not the case for women over 50. For over 50, exercise plays a much bigger role because your involuntary physical activity starts to drop. So, to make up for the drops of metabolism, you can actually exercise more It doesn't&nbsp;necessarily mean you have to do it hour seven days a week but frequency trumps everything else. If you&nbsp;were to do 10 minutes seven days a week it's far better than one hour once a week. However, what you eat, how much, and when is strictly&nbsp;controlled by you. So one thing I would also measure as a gauge of progress is your compliance&nbsp; to the keto diet. And your goal is to make it a game. Try to beat your compliance from previous&nbsp;week. So let's say you eat breakfast, lunch, and dinner seven days a week.That's 21 meals per week. So, if week one on the keto diet you could only stick to it 14 out of 21 meals we too try to shoot&nbsp; for 15. So, this is how you can stay motivated, even though you're not necessarily losing weight. The weight loss will come as a long-term effect. I have an entire video on intermittent fasting for women over 50, linked&nbsp; In the description below. In conclusion, what did we learn about the keto diet for women over&nbsp; 50? First of all, we learned what is the keto diet. The keto diet is a ketogenic diet, in other&nbsp;words it is a high-fat, low-carb moderate-protein diet. We also knew that it's very very important&nbsp;to get enough protein for the sake of, well, your muscles, as well as your bones. We also learned how&nbsp;to plan meals properly (on a keto diet), which is to figure out your macronutrients, to figure out the ingredients,&nbsp; to figure out what you're going to eat, and finally go shopping for those. Then, we also talked about&nbsp; exercise and the benefits of it. Improves strength, improve endurance improve flexibility, stronger bones&nbsp; and all the things that you want as a woman over 50. We also talked about how to monitor progress. That is, yeah you may want to use it for weight loss, but you also want to track your behaviors,&nbsp;as well as areas outside of just the nutrition side of things like improvements and strength,&nbsp; endurance, flexibility and so on. Now, what are my own personal views on the keto diet? I've been&nbsp;a personal trainer since 2006 and obviously I think there are many many ways to lose weight. One&nbsp;of them is a keto diet. I think the keto diet is highly overrated, but don't get me wrong. Overrated&nbsp;does not mean useless. The word overrated means benefits are being attributed to it that are not&nbsp;correct, but that does not mean it doesn't have any benefits. Useless means it has no benefit. I don't&nbsp;believe that either. I believe the keto diet is beneficial. Whom is it beneficial for? The people for&nbsp;whom is going to work. Who is it going to work for? It's going to work for you if you actually like&nbsp; the taste of keto foods. If you enjoy eggs, bacon, avocados, olive oil, things like that, the keto diet&nbsp; is likely a very very good choice for you and it's going to work for you well in the long term. The&nbsp;keto diet is not for you if you don't like those foods, so your long-term adherence is bad. There&nbsp;are also certain contraindications to the keto diet. If you have familial hypercholesterolemia or&nbsp; hyperlipidemia, it's not for you. What that means is that you have a genetic condition where your&nbsp; cholesterol is high no matter how well you eat or your triglycerides are high no matter how well you eat. It's probably not for you if you suffer from gout. It's not for you, if you've had&nbsp; your gallbladder removed. 4 reasons that are inherent to the keto diet. - Is The Keto Diet Good for Older Adults? women in their 50s these benefits include one reducing body fat many diets promise weight loss but in many cases that weight is mostly water keto increases fat burning and achieves better results than most other diets keto also preferentially targets belly fat which is actually called visceral fat and women over 50 belly fat tends to increase this increases the risk of heart disease cardiac arrest and stroke the accumulation of abdominal fat is largely due to the hormonal changes associated with the menopause two enhanced insulin sensitivity carbohydrates are digested and converted into glucose when carbohydrates are eaten the body produces the hormone insulin to transport glucose to the liver and muscles however as we age a body's sensitivity to insulin decreases which means glucose is more likely to be converted and stored as fat leading to weight gain a low carbohydrate diet increases insulin sensitivity this means that the few carbohydrates you eat are not converted into fat increased insulin sensitivity also helps regulate blood sugar levels low blood sugar levels are inextricably linked to better overall health and a lower risk of developing type 2 diabetes 3. improved brain function menopausal women often suffer from memory loss mood swings and difficulty concentrating they may even suffer from depression and anxiety this is because estrogen levels the main female sex hormone decreased during the menopause which affects the amount of glucose that reaches the brain fourth lower inflammation the aging process can be very taxing on the body menopausal women over 50 suffer from knee and hip pain as well as headaches and other non-specific ailments keto is a high fat diet and some fats are very helpful in reducing inflammation good anti-inflammatory fats that should be part of the keto diet include olive oil fatty fish such as sardines tuna and salmon avocados and avocado oil nuts in contrast foods such as refined carbohydrates sugar and processed foods are associated with increased these foods are not part of the keto diet 5. improved blood lipid levels many women in their 50s have elevated triglyceride and bad LDL cholesterol levels this is a recipe for heart disease despite their high fat content low carbohydrate diets have been shown to lower triglycerides and LDL cholesterol while increasing levels of good HDL cholesterol these changes are associated with improved cardiovascular health and a lower risk of heart disease 6. lowering blood pressure will intend to have lower blood pressure than men however this can change when you reach your 50s and the menopause sets in high blood pressure has been linked to a number of serious medical problems including heart disease kidney disease and strokes the low carbohydrate keto diet has been shown to help lower blood pressure 7. increased bone mass older women are prone to bone loss which can develop into osteoporosis if not stop this is a disease characterized by weak bones that are prone to fractures keto eliminates nutrients that can interfere with calcium absorption combined with plenty of green leafy vegetables which are naturally high in calcium keto can improve bone health and density eight less muscle loss women in their 50s tend to lose muscle faster than women in their 20s 30s and 40s muscle loss slows down metabolism leading to weight gain and making weight loss more difficult muscle loss also affects strength making everyday activities harder and more strenuous moderate amounts of protein are consumed on the ketogenic diet and protein is essential for muscle maintenance protein contains amino acids and amino acids are the building blocks of muscle tissue it is clear that keto can be very beneficial for women in their 50s both for weight loss and for improving health the keto diet means cutting out many of the foods we know are unhealthy and replacing them with foods that are high in beneficial nutrients

- 7 Day High Protein Diet Plan | Amazing Results | Dr Hansaji: Kind: captions Language: en Namaskar. If you observe closely, our modern Indian diet has become largely carbohydrate heavy and protein deficient. An average adult requires about 0.8 to 1 g of protein per kilogram body weight, which means roughly 50 to 60 g daily for many people. Yet, a large number of people consume far less, often around 30 to 40 g. This leads to fatigue, low immunity, and reduced strength. So, today I will share a simple 7-day high-protein diet plan using everyday Indian foods like dal, curd, sattu, paneer, and sprouts. These foods are affordable and already present in our kitchen. Let us now go day by day. Day one begins on a very grounded and sattvic way. In the morning, take five to six soaked almonds along with warm water. Soaking improves digestibility and supports better nutrient absorption. For breakfast, prepare two medium-size moong dal chilla using half cup soaked moong dal. This gives roughly 12 to 14 g of protein and 50 g paneer stuffing, which contributes another 8 to 9 g of protein. This breakfast itself provides close to 20 g of protein. Moong dal is considered one of the lightest and most digestible dal in yoga and Ayurveda. For lunch, have two medium roti from 60 g atta with one cup cooked mixed dal and one bowl curd. This combination provides approximately 18 to 20 g protein and supports gut health. In the evening, take 30 g roasted chana or a sattu drink. Sattu is famous in Bihar as a strength-giving food. For dinner, take one bowl vegetable moong dal khichdi and half cup sauteed green peas, adding another 12 to 14 g protein. Total for the day, approximately 55 to 60 g protein. Day two is about on variety while maintaining balance. In morning, continue with five soaked almonds for a steady start. For breakfast, prepare two besan chillas using half cup besan. This provides 10 to 11 g protein. Add vegetables like spinach or grated carrot to improve micronutrient intake. For lunch, take one cup cooked rajma with one cup brown rice and salad. This meal provides around 12 to 14 g of protein. Proper soaking for 8 to 10 hours ensures that rajma is well digested and does not feel heavy. In the evening, have one bowl sprout chaat from half cup sprouted moong, which gives 7 to 8 g protein. Add lemon to enhance mineral absorption. For dinner, have palak paneer made with 75 g paneer along with two small rotis, contributing to 12 to 14 g of protein. Throughout the day, you will get approximately 50 to 55 g protein. Day three, where consistency becomes important. So, continue with the plan. Begin with five to six soaked almonds to maintain continuity. For breakfast, prepare vegetable daliya with moong dal using half cup daliya and half cup moong dal. This provides about 10 to 12 g protein and keeps energy steady through morning. For lunch, have one cup chole with two multigrain roti with one bowl of curd. This gives approximately 18 to 20 g of protein. Combining cereals and pulses improves protein quality naturally. In the evening, take buttermilk with 1 tbsp sunflower or pumpkin seed, adding 4 to 5 g protein along with essential minerals. For dinner, take paneer or tofu stir-fry, about 75 g with vegetables and one roti, which gives 12 to 14 g protein. Total for the day, you will have taken around 50 g protein. At this stage, your body starts responding, energy becomes steadier, and cravings reduced. Staying consistent here brings visible benefits. Day four continues with light and nourishing combination. Start the day with soaked almond as before. For breakfast, have one cup sprouted moong salad. This gives 10 to 12 g protein. Sprouting enhances nutrient availability and makes digestion easier. For lunch, take two idlis with one big bowl sambar made with about 30 g mixed dals. This meal provides 10 to 12 g protein. Fermented food like idlis supports gut health and improve nutrient absorption. In the evening, drink sattu mixed with buttermilk, providing 6 to 7 g protein and a cooling effect on the system. For dinner, have one cup mixed dal with lauki or turai sabzi and two rotis, adding about 50 g protein. Your total protein intake for this day will be approximately 45-50 g. Day five brings familiar comfort foods in a balanced way. Begin with five soaked almonds. For breakfast, prepare one paneer stuffed paratha using 75 g paneer served with curd. This gives 15 to 18 g protein. When prepared with moderate ghee, it remains nourishing and balanced. For lunch, have green peas pulao with one cup peas and raita. This provides around 10 to 12 g of protein. Green peas are often underestimated but contributes meaningfully to daily intake. In the evening, take 30 g roasted peanuts or chana, adding 6 to 7 g protein. For dinner, take moong dal soup with vegetable salad, which gives another 10 to 12 g protein. Total protein for the day will be approximately 45-50 g. Day six supports strength while keeping digestion light. Start again with five soaked almonds. For breakfast, have oats cooked in 250 ml milk topped with nuts and seeds, about 10-15 g. This provides about 10 to 15 g protein along with the sustained energy. For lunch, take one cup masoor dal with rotis and vegetables, gives around 15 g protein. Masoor dal is quick to cook and rich in iron. In the evening, have sprouted moong or boiled chana salad, adding 7 to 8 g protein. Dinner includes paneer bhurji with 75 g paneer and one roti and salads, giving 12 14 g protein. Total for the day would be approximately 50 g protein. Finally, we come to day seven, which will make your system feel light and nourished. Begin with five soaked almonds. For breakfast, take two idlis with sambar, providing around 10 g protein. For lunch, have one cup mixed dal with brown rice and a bowl of curd, giving 18 to 20 g protein. This meal is simple yet deeply nourishing. In the evening, drink sattu buttermilk, adding 6 to 7 g protein. Dinner includes light moong dal khichdi with vegetables, giving 10 to 12 g protein. You will have taken approximately 45 g protein throughout the day. Across all seven days, protein intake stays consistently between 45 to 60 g daily, fulfilling your body's requirement naturally. And what we see here is very clear. This is not a special diet. This is simply our traditional Indian food arranged with awareness. Now, you may notice something important in this entire plan. We did not rely on one heavy protein meal. Instead, we distributed protein throughout the day. This is because your body absorbs and utilizes protein better when it is spread across meals rather than taken all at once. But, why is protein so important from a scientific viewpoint? Proteins have amino acids, which are the building blocks of muscles, enzymes, hormones, and immune cells. After the age of 30, your body naturally begins to lose muscle mass slowly every decade. If protein intake is low, this process speeds up. That is why many people in their 40s and 50s complain of weakness, joint instability, and fatigue. Ayurveda also says the same thing. Protein-rich food helps nourish mass dhatu, the muscle tissue. The mass dhatu is strong, posture improves, metabolism improves, and even immunity becomes stronger. Ayurveda also reminds us that digestion, that is agni, jatharagni, must be strong. If digestion is weak, even nutritious food may not be fully utilized. So, for this, I will give you a few tips. Soak pulses and legumes before cooking. This reduces compounds that interfere with digestion. Add jeera, ginger, or hing while cooking dals. These spices help digestion and reduce bloating. Try to combine grains and pulses. For example, rice with dal, roti with chana. Together, they provide a better balance of amino acids. Eat protein foods earlier in the day when digestion is stronger. Along with these steps, I must also tell you about some precautions. Avoid trying to increase protein suddenly in very large amount. Increase gradually to give time for your digestive system to adapt. Drink enough water throughout the day. People with kidney disease should consult doctor before increasing protein intake. Also, remember protein alone is not enough. We still need vegetables, healthy fats, and adequate fiber. Now, I'll share with you a simple high-protein laddu recipe that you can eat as a daily snack. This laddu is easy to make and you can store it for up to a week. Take 1 cup roasted chana powder. Add half cup powdered roasted peanuts or almonds. Add 2 Tbsp sesame seed. Add three to four chopped dates or a small amount of jaggery powder for sweetness. Then, warm 2 Tbsp of ghee. Add one to two tsp flaxseed powder. Mix all the dry ingredients well. Pour it into this mixture and then bind it into small small laddus. Each laddu provides nice combination of protein, high fats, and minerals. One laddu in the afternoon with herbal tea can be a nourishing snack. You must understand that health is not built up in a single meal or a single week. It is built slowly by the choices you make. When you choose balanced food, when you eat with awareness, when you maintain movement through yoga and walking, your body naturally begins to regain strength. And remember, protein is not only about just muscles. It supports immunity, energy, recovery, and graceful aging. So, from today, add natural protein in your daily meals and allow your body to maintain its strength.

My wife and I will prepare a bunch of chicken breasts, leave it in the refrigerator so she, myself, and the boys can easily access it during lunches and even dinners and make it very easy to get a healthy protein-rich snack. Next in the category is one of my absolute favorites, and that is bone broth. Bone broth is a direct bioavailable form of collagen. consuming it gives your body exactly what it needs to create and build collagen as well as hydrate the skin and just give areas outside of the skin, joints, everything else all those important things that it needs to function better. So, there's been a lot of discussion about collagen peptides using it as a supplement. We've talked about that in the past, but if you're using bone broth, you don't need any of that. You're getting all of it. High protein, very low calorie source of those essential amino acids. So, how does Neta use this? Every day after her workouts, at 10:00 a.m., she has her bone broth prepared, which she makes in the morning. It's a great post-workout protein source that she drinks and nourishes her body and gives the body back the amino acids and protein that it needs while rehydrating. And to this, she adds ginger and turmeric and some mushrooms and even some kelp noodles. And we'll talk about spices in just a moment. All right, next up, let's talk about foods that protect the skin. We're talking about antioxidants and vitamin C. The skin is in constant bombardment of UVA, pollutants, toxins, everything that is causing cellular degradation and oxidative stress. Now, these are all just fancy terms for basically the tissues need protection and need antioxidants to soak up this damage and prevent the cells from going down a path that ultimately leads to cell death or apoptosis. This is where antioxidants and vitamin C are literally non-negotiables. Now, ingesting vitamin C is important for two reasons. Number one, all of the collagen molecules that you produce require vitamin C as a important co-actor during this process. So vitamin C is necessary in our diets to build collagen. Many people don't realize that in addition to zinc, which we'll talk about in a moment. But you also need topical vitamin C that helps directly get into the skin and form those antioxidant protection. This is why topical vitamin C is also a non-negotiable. But the oral form a lot of people forget about because as we get older, we stop taking in foods that have vitamin C, etc. But if you're trying to stimulate collagen, which is the name of the game of any anti-aging routine, you need this around so that you can properly build collagen. One of the easiest and most delicious and best ways to get vitamin C is through berries. So blueberries, strawberries, these are key things that give you tons of antioxidants and vitamin C at the same time. Neta is always infusing her drinking water with strawberries as well as mint, but also always has some cut strawberries and lots of blueberries sitting around in the refrigerator ready to just basically grab and go. What these foods do is they introduce the bacteria that's necessary to be in the gut and create the biome that will ultimately lead to decreased inflammation and more healthy looking skin. But these bacteria need to eat something. They actually have foods that they need which are prebiotic. So probiotics is the actual bacteria. Prebiotic is the food that the bacteria actually eat. So what are some prebiotic foods that are great for the gut? So, chia seeds, green leafy vegetables, as well as avocados are some of the best prebiotic foods you can eat. Another super healthy form of prebiotics is artichokes. Believe it or not, high in fiber, tons of very beneficial prebiotic source of food for the probiotic biome that you have worked on developing. So, as mentioned, Neta takes a probiotic in the mornings in the way of sauerkraut. When you lift a heavy weight to failure and you can't do it anymore, that creates lots of little tears in the proteins in your muscles and then your body's like, "Oh my god, we have to go repair that." So your body brings all these extra amino acids and goes and repairs that muscle that was broken and makes it stronger than before. That is how you build up muscle by exerting force, tearing it down, and then your body will build it back up stronger. This is why it is so important to lift weights. And females out there, I know it's not something we're used to doing. I know we think that lifting weights is just for men. It's not the case. Okay? Okay. The belief that women should not be lifting weights is so antiquated. It's like stuck in the '9s where we thought all we had to do was just like cardio for hours on a treadmill. It's so key to lift weights. I lift weights three times a week. And let me tell you, it is impossible to look like a bodybuilder when you're female. It's very, very difficult. I've been working on my biceps. Can I show you here? Yeah. I've been working on my biceps here for like years and they're still tiny. Okay. But but lifting weights will help you build stronger muscles and increase your muscle mass a little bit. Not so much. Maybe a couple pounds of muscle mass per year, if at all. It's very, very difficult. But in the meantime, you'll get toned. You'll feel strong. Your back won't hurt. You'll feel good when you walk around. You know, it's just it's a feeling you get from within. When you have good muscle mass and you feel strong, it increases your confidence in so many different aspects of life, actually. So once you've tear down your muscle, how do you actually build it? So as I explained, your body is going to be looking for amino acids to build back up your muscle. But if you want to really build your muscle mass, you have to be eating protein. Now, here's the problem. Most of us do not eat enough protein. The current recommendation in the US is8 gram of protein per kilo of body weight per day. That means that if you're 70 kilos like me, you should be eating 56 grams of protein per day, which is three eggs, one cup of lentils, and half a yogurt. The thing is, this recommendation is not at all adequate anymore, and science is proving it. This could be enough protein to not die, but it's not enough to maintain your muscle mass, and it is not enough to build your muscle mass. So over the last few years, scientists have realized that actually you should be aiming for two grams of protein per kilo per day or one gram of protein per pound of body weight per day. Again, if you click in the description of this episode, you'll see a PDF that will help you calculate how much you need. So essentially, we need to double and add a little bit more to that protein recommendation. One study that was published in nutrients is called is it time to reconsider the US recommendations for dietary protein and amino acid intake and they showed that in older adults specifically you need to be eating at least 1.6 grams of protein per kilo of body weight per day just to not lose muscle mass. You need to be eating twice the recommendation just to not lose muscle mass as you age. So for me, I try to get two grams per kilo of body weight per day, which is kind of the same as one gram per pound of body weight per day. Now, the thing is, once you calculate this, you're going to freak out. You're going to be like, whoa, that's way more protein than I expected. Yes, 100%. The thing is protein intake is so important because your body cannot make all these amino acids from within. It needs to get them from the outside to be able to build all the things it needs to build out of them plus maintain and grow muscle mass. So for example, for me, my objective is about 140 grams of protein per day. That's equivalent to 20 eggs or 23 cups of Greek yogurt or seven cups of cooked lentils. It's hard to get, I know, but of course, if you add some fish and some meat in there, it becomes much, much, much easier, which is what I do. So, again, have a look in the PDF in the description of this episode. I've put together a list of foods and their protein content. But what you need to know is you're not eating enough protein at every meal. You need to be prioritizing it for your health today, but also for your health as you age. It's so important. So, when is the best time to eat your protein? Well, interestingly, the best time to eat your protein is throughout the day in three or four sittings because any protein that is not used is going to be excreted through your urine. So, in terms of helping your body assimilate more of the protein that you eat, it's better to have it during every single meal and snack versus all at once and then no more protein at all. And should you be having protein after you workout? Yes, this is a great time to eat protein because your muscles are looking for amino acids to rebuild themselves. However, you don't have to stress. There's an old belief that you should be, you know, downing a protein shake within 10 minutes after the end of a workout. Now, we know you actually have way more time. You have hours after your workout to still be able to assimilate protein. So after a workout, great moment, but you could just wait until you get home and have food. You don't have to down a protein shake just as you're finishing your gym session. But if you want to, you can. Now, can you eat too much protein? There is an amount of protein that would be bad for your kidneys if you ate all of that every single day. And the upper limit is 3.7 grams of protein per kilo of body weight per day. Now, that's about 260 gram of protein per day for me, which is 43 eggs per day. So, it's really hard to eat too much protein. I think that would be close to impossible. So, you really don't have to worry about that unless you're eating just 5 lbs of steak every hour, you're going to be fine. Now, if you don't eat animal protein, you have to know that plant protein sources contain less of a few essential amino acids, methionine, lysine, and tryptophan. So, it's going to be harder to get those, and you have to be really conscious of making sure you're getting enough of those amino acids, which means that if you're vegan, it's possible to get enough protein. It just takes a lot of work and planning. That's why adding some eggs or some dairy is so beneficial to get your protein intake if you've started from a point where you're vegan. So potentially consider that so that you get enough protein. Otherwise, you just have to be really conscious and informed and make sure you're eating lots and lots of tofu and lentils and pulses and peas at every meal in the right combinations to be able to get the amino acids that you need. And one last note on protein powder. So protein powder is a great way to add protein throughout your day. Just want to make sure of the source. So, for example, whey is going to be better and more complete than soy protein. Also, be careful with the protein powders that are flavored. Any chocolate or vanilla stuff can actually contain some heavy metal traces. So, prefer unsweetened, unflavored protein powders and then add your own flavoring yourself when you're making your shake. That's the recommendation. So, in conclusion, increasing protein intake is so important if you're an athlete and you're trying to build muscle mass because if you don't eat enough protein, you're not going to build any muscle mass. it's just not going to work. And second, if you're just a regular person, not an athlete, who wants to live well and not die young and not be frail when you age. So increasing our protein intake should be the number one thing we think about. And the good thing is when you prioritize protein, you also feel much more satiated. You don't feel as hungry, have fewer cravings. There's a really interesting scientific theory called the protein leverage hypothesis that says that your body will keep you hungry until you've given it enough protein to eat. So, a really easy switch you can do is try to start your day with a proteinrich breakfast, which is what I recommend in my savory breakfast. So just adding a bunch of protein to your breakfast is a really efficient way to not only keep your glucose levels steady, but also get some protein in the morning to help your body get the signal that hey, there's protein here. We're going to be fine. You can download some free savory breakfast recipes in the description of this episode. So to recap, get the PDF to calculate how much protein you need and to see all the foods and how much protein is in each of the foods. and also get the savory breakfast recipes started out. Let me know how it goes and I'll see you next time. Big kiss.

- Best high-protein foods for energy, gut health and longevity (and how I get 30g per meal): Kind: captions Language: en If you're looking to eat more protein, it can feel really tricky to go from 15 g of protein to 30 g of protein without relying on protein powders or lots of meat at every meal time. If you're new here, I'm a medical doctor and nutritionist, and my mission is to help you improve your health with food. So, I'm not against eating meat at all, but if you want to stay healthier for longer, it's best to get a mix of different proteins with the majority of them coming from plants. Now, why is that? Plants aren't just protein. They contain an array of other nutrients that are super healthy for us. One being fiber that you tend not to get in animal-based proteins, as well as other micronutrients and phytonutrients that can lower inflammation, lower your cholesterol, and keep your heart in good order for longer. For every 3% extra plant protein in your diet, it reduces your chances of chronic disease and helps you live longer. But it doesn't mean giving up meat. It's more about shifting the balance of your proteins more towards plants. Personally, I aim for around 70% coming from plant-based protein or the rest coming from animal-based proteins. I don't believe you should be having as many eggs as you want every single day. I think they can form part of a really healthy diet, particularly when you are diversifying your protein sources. That's like a luxury occasion. For poultry, I'm looking for breast or chicken thigh without the skin. Fish is kind of like a free-for-all. The fats that fish contain are generally omega-3 fatty acids. They're generally anti-inflammatory. And the ones I personally go for are oily fish. So things like anchovies, herring, sardines, salmon, and some white fish like monkfish. But you might have heard that meat or animal-based proteins are higher quality than plant-based proteins. Let's break that down. So whilst this is true, when you look at protein absorption scores, animal-based proteins tend to be higher in terms of the bioavailability of these building blocks of protein called essential amino acids, of which there are nine essential ones. The thing about animal-based proteins is that they also tend to be higher in saturated fat and lower in fiber. So whilst they might be great on those individual scores, we want to be looking at our diet a lot more holistically. Plant-based proteins on the other hand tend to be low in a couple of these amino acids, which means that they score lower across these different scoring mechanisms. It's kind of like judging the team based on the weakest player. If it's low in just one of those amino acids, the whole plant-based protein gets marked down. In reality, what you need to be focusing on is getting enough total protein from multiple sources. This way, you don't need to rely heavily on just animal-based proteins, and you get all the other benefits of adding some plant-based proteins that we mentioned earlier. So, as someone that needs to be consuming 30 g plus of protein at every single meal, having 300 g of any one of these products is going to be a lot of product, a lot of fiber, and something that my gut probably wouldn't be able to handle. But, of course, we're not just relying on beans and lentils for our protein sources. Moving further into the pantry section, we've got things like grains. We are combining multiple different proteins rather than just relying on one. We want to start with a core protein source. This is doing the bulk of the work. It's giving us the most bang for our buck. We're not just going to rely on one of these. This could be your lean meat, your fish, your eggs, or your plant-based protein like tofu or tempeh. And they're not just adding protein, but they're also going to be adding all those other nutrients, mainly fiber. Think about it. You're having a spaghetti bolognese, add a tin of lentils.
