- How To Make My Anti-Aging Meals (Live to 120+): Kind: captions Language: en Do not die. >> Hi friends. Given that we are the first generation who won't die, my team and I have scoured the scientific literature looking for the evidence on how to build the best nutrition protocol. The rule is every calorie has to fight for its life. >> Round one, fight. Every day I consume three meals and the meals vary daytoday, but what they do have in common is a set of foods that we have deemed to be the superfoods of all things that I could put into my body. Today, I'm going to share with you how my personal chef prepares three of these dishes. While the recipes change, each meal must meet the following criteria. They have to be the most elite ingredients for longevity. It needs to keep me within my daily macros of 25% protein, 35% carbohydrates, and 40% fat. The ingredients need to be included in the don't diet food guide. And obviously, it can't include the bad things such as sugars, fried foods, etc. You would be surprised, and maybe you're not, of how frequently you find these things hiding in your food. My chef also prepares the food in a way where we can make it one day and have enough food for 3 days. All right, let's get started with sweet potato curry. This dish meets the criteria of foods from the Don't Die Food Guide, including macadamia nuts, cauliflower, turmeric. It's also great because you can make it in under 30 minutes. First, chop one whole onion. There's no correct way of doing this, but I aim for a fine dice. Once done, take a medium stainless steel pot and put it on mediumigh heat. It's crucial that your pots are stainless steel or ceramic, as non-stick cookware releases forever chemicals known as PAS into your food. These can stick around for decades, messing with your metabolism, hormones, and fertility. Once your pot is heated up, add in half a tablespoon of avocado oil. Use avocado oil when cooking because it has a higher smoke point. This will form fewer toxic carcinogens than other oils during cooking. Keep it on medium heat to make sure the oil does not exceed 375° F or 190° C. Once the oil starts to simmer, add in your onion. Stir and carry on cooking until it's nice and translucent. It's going to be about 5 minutes. As the onion cooks, use a spoon to peel your ginger. This is faster than using a knife or peeler and way more efficient because you don't remove any of the juicy flesh underneath. Next, peel a couple of cloves of garlic and chop them with the ginger into a fine mince. Let it cook with your onion for a few minutes. There should be an amazing smell in your kitchen right now. While the aromatics are cooking, peel your sweet potatoes and chop into cubes. Sweet potatoes have more fiber than regular potatoes, so you absorb their natural sugars slower. This reduces blood sugar spikes and keeps you feeling fuller for longer. Add to your pot. Then, peel and chop your carrots into rounds. Add them to the pot as well. Giving everything a nice stir. Okay, more vegetables. Time to chop your broccoli and cauliflower. There are two things to keep in mind here. First, use the stock and the fuates because the stock adds a nice earthy taste and has more iron, calcium, vitamin C, and fiber. Second, and this is an odd one, for the best health results, let the broccoli and cauliflower sit for 40 minutes after chopping before you cook it. This is because chopping activates a natural enzyme in broccoli and cauliflower called mirrosynise, which then unlocks sulfurophane, one of the strongest anti-cancer compounds you can find in food. It also has potential sugar-lowering detoxifying properties. But heat from cooking stops this process. So chop at the start of your cooking session and wait a bit to let your vegetable reach maximum sulfurophane levels. So we have all our aromatics in the pot, including ginger, garlic, and onion, which are the base of every good curry. Now we'll add in the black pepper and a teaspoon of turmeric. We're adding black pepper because it boosts the power of the turmeric. A compound in pepper called piperin slows the breakdown of curcumin in your gut and liver. This makes it more stable inside your body and makes its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and metabolic benefits last longer. Give everything a good stir to incorporate all the flavors. Next, add in your broccoli, rested or unrested, and cauliflower, and pour in enough water to cover everything, giving it a nice stir. Now bring up to a boil and then cover the pot and let it gently simmer for 15 minutes. While that's coming to a boil, we'll make our quinoa. Heat up a mediumsized pot. Add in two cups of vegetable stock and 1 cup of quinoa. Once that comes up to a boil, lower the heat and simmer for 15 minutes. Now juice two limes and reserve the juice until you finish cooking. You always want to add lemon or lime juice at the end of your stews or curries because if you do it too early, it loses the citrus flavors you want in your dish. Now, we're going to chop our trimmed green beans into bite-sized pieces. Place to the side and chop your cilantro. [Music] Now that the quinoa is finished cooking after 15 minutes, turn off the heat and let it sit aside covered. Next, we'll add in the rest of our ingredients to the curry. peas, green beans, cilantro, citrus juice. Finally, macadamia nutmilk will give your curry a really nice creamy flavor and color. Macadamia is rich in monounsaturated fats and can lower blood sugar and bad cholesterol. Bring that up to a quick boil and then turn off the heat and set aside. Now it's time for plating. I think the plating is just as important as the cooking. Precise, intentional arrangements stimulate the appetite and encourage you to eat slower and more mindfully, which all improves digestion and nutrition absorption. To plate, give the quinoa a nice little fluff with your fork. Add to the plate as a base. Top with the curry and add plenty of curry broth because that's where all the flavor is. Garnish with black sesame seeds, cilantro, and micro greens. And if you're feeling extra bougie, you can add in some edible flowers. Finish with a wedge of lime and enjoy. Bona petit. If you're meal prepping, keep the curry and quinoa in separate containers. Then reheat and combine when you're ready to eat. >> Next up is the green smoothie. I love this dish because it's like a summertime palette cleanser. >> It's loaded with healthy ingredients and tastes absolutely delicious. You might have heard that you shouldn't consume fruit in juice form because it strips away all the fiber and lets your body absorb sugar unnaturally fast. This is all true, but it doesn't apply to smoothies. The key difference is that for smoothies, you don't remove the solid parts of the fruits and vegetables. You just blend them all together. This means you still get their natural fiber content, which regulates sugar absorption, feeds your gut microbiota, and prevents inflammation. In fact, adding vegetables, nuts, and fats to your smoothie dilutes the sugar content of the fruit, slowing down absorption even more and keeping you full for longer. All right, let's get going. First, add in your macadamia nut. Adding the liquid first stops your ingredients from stalling the blender. Next, add in one banana, frozen if possible, to thicken the consistency of the smoothie. Then add in the rest of your ingredients. Mac nuts, three frozen cherries, pomegranate seeds, hemp seeds, wheat grass, vanilla extract, chia seeds, juice of half of a lemon, and one celery stock. These were all chosen for a reason. Pomegranates have unique polyphenols that protects your cholesterol from oxidation, which all improves your blood flow. Dark cherries have another distinct type of elite polyphenols that help with cardiovascular health and are rich in melatonin, which helps with sleep. Hemp seeds are a great source of essential omega-3 and omega6 fat with a remarkable 32% protein content. Close the lid and give everything a good blend for 30 seconds. Next, add in a handful or two of washed spinach and blend again until all your ingredients are thoroughly mixed. Take your glass and serve your smoothie. Note that this will be a thicker smoothie. If you want to make it less thick, you can always add in more filtered water or your choice of nutmilk. Top off with a little strawberry and mint and you're ready to go. This is a great meal prep smoothie. You can make a big batch and store in the fridge in an airtight container for up to 3 to 4 days. Just make sure to give it a quick stir or shake before enjoying. >> Finally, we have the roasted vegetable, chickpea, and lentil bowl. I love this dish because I have learned to absolutely love vegetables and legumes. Roasting makes the vegetables sweet and juicy. The lentils are packed with protein, and the creamy chickpea dressing has a great hit of high polyphenol olive oil. Of our three recipes, this might be the most satisfying to eat. Preheat your oven to 400° and start chopping the vegetables. We'll start with half of a cauliflower and cut into bite-sized pieces. Keep the other half in your fridge for later, or you can double the recipe to make more portions. Set this aside in a mixing bowl and chop up one zucchini. Now, take your red bell pepper, cut the top and bottom like this. Cut in the middle and slice out the bitter white membrane before chopping into bite-sized pieces. Once you're done chopping vegetables, measure about 1 teaspoon of macadamia nut oil or any kind of oil with a high smoke point like avocado. Take a tiny bit of this oil and spread onto your baking sheet to prevent sticking when the vegetables are in the oven. Then add the rest of your oil to your bowl of mixed vegetables. Give everything a good toss and add to your baking sheet. Spread evenly and place in the oven at 400° for about 15 minutes. Now that the vegetables are in the oven, we'll start cooking our black lentils. Lentils are 18% protein, but also 16% fiber, which again slows down food absorption, making you fuller for longer and reducing blood sugar spikes. Studies suggest that eating 10 g of legume fibers every day lowers colon cancer risk by 32% versus just 10% for the same amount of other dietary fibers. Add about 1 and 1/2 cups of cold water and 3/4 of a cup of black lentils. Bring up to a boil, then partially cover with a pot lid and turn down to a medium low temp. We're going to let this simmer for about 15 minutes. Next, we're going to sauté our shiakei mushrooms. We're using shiakei because they have three particularly healthy compounds. Aergoionine, lentinine, and aerotadin. Bring your stainless steel pan up to a really high heat for a few minutes so that the pan becomes non-stick. I like to take a teaspoon of water and splash it onto the heated stainless steel pan. If the water forms beads, that means that your pan is officially non-stick and ready for cooking. Place the shiakei mushrooms in the preheated pan with a teaspoon of avocado oil or any type of high smoke point oil. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Once cooked, take your mushrooms off the heat and set aside. Now, let's make our dressing. Take a cup of cooked chickpeas, 1 tbsp of nutritional yeast, 1 tbsp of olive oil, one garlic clove, a tablespoon of lemon juice, 4 tbsp of filtered water, and a handful of chives. Put everything in the blender and blend for 30 seconds. High polyphenol olive oil is the most powerful ingredient in this dressing. It increases HDL or good cholesterol and reduces several key markers of cardiovascular disease. We use blueprint olive oil as it has a very high polyphenol count. Garlic has also been shown to lower blood pressure and help the immune system. Once your lentils and vegetables have been cooked, take everything off the heat and set to cool. Plate up by placing your lentils in a shallow bowl. Next, take your roasted vegetables, sauteed mushrooms, and a handful or two of kale. Add our dressing to the mixing bowl and give everything a nice toss. Put a big helping of vegetable mix on your lentils. Add in a large pinch of hemp seeds and a wedge of lemon. And that's it. - MAJOR Changes to High Protein Diet in 2026 [New Guide To Lose Fat Faster]: Kind: captions Language: en As we've gotten more and more savvy with how to use protein to achieve a weight loss goal, there have been some major changes that you really need to be aware of, especially if you want to maximize for your fat loss results while also feeling great and fueling your overall wellness journey, too. So, today I'm sharing eight major changes to eating a higher protein diet that you need to be aware of so that you can continue or start seeing those fat loss, wellness, and gut health benefits. Now, if you've been watching my channel for a while, then this idea is not very new to you. However, it is new to a lot of people. And protein pacing is the idea that you spread your protein evenly out between three meals. This is rather than having like a couple of meals be really high in protein and others being low in protein or having six small meals that are spread throughout the day that have an even amount of protein. By focusing on three meals that are evenly rich in protein, it really helps to maximize for fat loss, satiety, and gut health. It makes us feel really full and very satisfied, which makes it so easy to not snack on foods that won't support your goals. But on top of that, by properly spacing out your meals in this way and not snacking in between, it also gives you the double benefit of really ramping up gut cleaning and reducing bloating. And that's because when we don't eat for exactly 3 hours and 45 minutes, it turns on the gut cleaning process called the migrating motor complex. This flushes out left behind food and bacteria that would otherwise cause bloating. So, the key thing here is to really watch out for breakfast because breakfast for a lot of people tends to be the lowest in protein, especially when you consider foods like cereal or toast or even sneakier items like protein bars or eggs. Second thing that's really important for you to know is to try and eat eggs, dairy, or meat at your first meal, aka breakfast. These are the proteins that are highest in a type of amino acid called leucine. These also just so happen to be the most satiating proteins. Lucine helps to really kickstart muscle recovery and creation, which also helps us to prevent muscle loss. And when we're focusing on a fat loss journey, we really, really need to make sure that we're preventing muscle loss because as we lose muscle, it slows down our metabolism and makes it harder to maintain or even continue seeing fat loss results. It's not to say low fat, but instead making sure that he actually hit his protein needs before then adding in fat. Because it is really easy to accidentally eat a lot more fat before you actually get enough protein, especially if you're eating a lot of foods like sausage or salami or if you're using a lot of ingredients like peanut butter and almond butter. So, choosing really high quality, dense proteins and focusing on those first before then adding in other fats is a great strategy to make sure that you're covering your bases. Okay. My husband uses it for joints and other structural aspects, but as a plant-based individual, I don't use collagen. There is a confusion between collagen protein and something like a normal protein powder. Collagen protein is not what we think of when we're using post exercise protein because it's a structural protein. It does not go into the quantification of your dietary protein. So if we're looking at collagen, there's two kinds. We have a native collagen and a peptide. A native collagen when ingested has an interaction with your immune system to reduce the inflammation and the signaling to degrade your cartilage. We see peptides are small bits of that whole collagen that are actually digested and those small bits of peptides go to the target tissue and are used to regenerate some of the cartilage but also to trigger a response to stop degrading the cartilage. So, it's a structural protein. If you want good hair, you want good skin, you want good nails, you want good joints, you're going for collagen. If you want muscle, muscle protein synthesis, nerve, bone, all of the things for dietary protein, you go for a protein supplement that is plant-based or whey isolate. The other must-haves really is an omega-3. It's really important for women who are super active. If we look at a cellular aspect of increasing our oxidative and antioxidative status, omega-3s are really essential for brain health, cellular health, and so it's one of my go-tos for sure. And then we get to adaptogens. These are two that I use on the regular. I use rodeiola and ashagandha. So I'll take a step back and tell you what an adaptogen is. An adaptogen is a plant compound that has an effect in the body that we don't normally see with a pharmaceutical. The best way I can explain it is if we look at seaweed and we see seaweed changes different colors. When it is in a dark cold part of the ocean, it has a higher protein content and it in itself is dark. When that same plant comes up closer to the surface in the summer months, it becomes lighter in color and has a reduced protein composition. The reason for that is it's adapting to the environment. So a plant in itself can adapt to different situations to different stressors. So there are certain plants that grow in really environmentally challenging conditions. So we can look at MKA, we can look at ashagandha, we can look at rodeiola. They adapt to the harsh environment that it's in. And because it can adapt, that same compound has a response to our human stress system. So if we look at something like rodeiola, it has multi-factoral effect on the body. We can see that it modulates estrogen for perry and post-menopausal women. So that means it either increases the sensitivity of your receptors or it decreases the sensitivity based on what your own individual body needs. It also has an effect on cortisol. It modulates your cortisol receptors as well. So, it helps with that tired but wired feeling by dropping cortisol's effect on the body so that you can get into more of a parasympathetic state. When I'm traveling with jet lag, I use it to help me get into a parasympathetic state for sleeping so that it can help my body reset its circadian rhythm and get me out of a jet lag state. With ashagandha, it's an overall stress reducer because it has a direct effect on cortisol cortisol responses, but it also has an effect on thyroid. So, it does have a contraindication for individuals who are using any kind of thyroid medication because again its adaptogenic response is it understands how stressed your body is from a metabolic as well as an autonomic nervous system. So, it can adjust your thyroid hormone sensitivity, but it also affects cortisol and how your body responds to cortisol. So, it's another one if you're going into a highly stressed situation or you're living a highly stressed life, it helps modulate that stress to reduce that overarching tired but wired feeling. So, that would be the cadre of supplements that I use on the regular. And when we're looking at all of our individual basis of when do we use supplements, they are actually that a supplement. We want to look at real food first, but sometimes there are holes in our diet where we need to fill and that's where some supplementation can come into play. Or it might be that we don't want to go onto a true westernized pharmaceutical so that we look to use adaptogens to help modulate some of the effects that a pharmaceutical would. But again, it's an individual journey. It's an individual basis. I'm just sharing with what I use and working with companies that I trust. - Dermatologist: Do Collagen Peptides REALLY WORK for Anti-Aging?: Kind: captions Language: en if you've ever wondered whether taking collagen supplements can actually make a difference in your Skin's health and appearance you're not alone today let's talk about oral collagen supplements what they are what they do and whether they actually live up to the anti-aging hype collagen is a protein that acts as scaffolding or structural support for your skin it's what helps keep your skin firm and plump when you're young but as we age Even in our 20s collagen production goes down by about 1% a year this leads to saggy skin and wrinkles as we age this known drop in collagen production as we age is why collagen is such a buzzword in Anti-aging products and supplements collagen supplements typically come in powder form capsules or in liquid form and most are hydrolized collagen peptides which means that the collagen is broken down into smaller pieces basically pre-digested little bits of collagen when you take collagen supplements they're broken down into even smaller pieces amino acids in your gut some people argue that these amino acids are no different from from what you'd get from eating protein rich foods like chicken and fish but there's some research to suggest that there's more to the story there is some evidence in humans that collagen peptides can improve skin hydration and elasticity I've been taking collagen peptides for about 6 months now and to be honest I usually buy whatever is on sale at Costco but there are a couple of things that I don't love about taking collagen peptides number one they're expensive take this product as an example the recommended daily serving size is 20 G and and the container has 22 servings it retails for about $30 us that means you're spending 1.36 per day just for the powder number two The Taste let's just say that a couple of the bigname brands taste the way a hamster cage smells not ideal right given these issues I wanted to see if it was actually worth it for me to continue buying and taking these products a quick side note for vegetarians and vegans collagen supplements usually aren't plant-based most are derived from animal sources es like the Bones skin tendons and cartilage of cows and pigs most of the clinical studies that have been performed on collagen supplements have been based on collagen from animal sources there aren't comparable options or evidence for plant-based Alternatives at this time so what does the science say while not all studies are created equal there are some randomized control trials suggesting that oral collagen can improve hydration and elasticity a 2021 meta analysis which included data from over 1700 patients concluded that collagen supplements led to statistically significant improvements in skin elasticity and hydration after 8 to 12 weeks of consistent use it's worth noting though that many of the studies on collagen supplements are funded by the companies producing these supplements while this doesn't automatically invalidate the research it's something that I keep in mind one thing that surprised me when reviewing studies on collagen supplements is how little the doses of collagen peptides were in many of the studies as little as 2.5 grams a day I did not find strong eviden to suggest that consuming more collagen peptides like the 20 gram per day recommended by both of the powders I tried from Costco leads to better results for example take this study sponsored by a German company that makes a collagen drink called elastin I have no conflict of interest it contains just 2.5 gram of collagen peptides along with acerola fruit extract vitamin C vitamin E Biotin and zinc despite the small dose the study which was reasonably welld designed randomized 72 women to either the drink or a placebo which did not contain the collagen or extra nutrients that I mentioned after 12 weeks the group taking elastin showed objective improvements in both skin hydration and elasticity 2.5 gram of collagen peptides from from one brand isn't necessarily equivalent to 2.5 gram from another the amino acid content can vary depending on the animal Source like Bine or marine and the specific tissue used skin versus cartilage unfortunately most brands don't disclose the exact tissue Source but at least matching the animal Source When comparing products is a decent place to start before you jump on the collagen train here are a few considerations results take time most benefits are seen after at least 8 weeks of daily use it's not a miracle cure while collagen can help it's just one piece of the puzzle I consider sun protection a healthy diet and not smoking to be way more important than collagen supplements to My overall health including my Skin's Health cost collagen supplements aren't cheap and their effects might not justify the expense for everyone so does oral collagen supplementation work the answer is maybe there's some evidence pointing toward modest benefit in skin hydration elasticity and texture if you're curious and you're not vegan or vegetarian it might be worth trying a collagen peptide supplement for two to three months to see if you notice a difference me personally based on my deep dive this is a low-risk supplement studies on collagen peptides used for anti-aging or for arthritis pain have consistently shown that collagen supplements are well tolerated given that there's some evidence of benefit I'll continue to take my collagen supplements that said I'll be reducing the amount that I take from 20 g a day down to 5 g a day until I see evidence that there is a real dose dependent benefit to taking the greater amount that will reduce my cost per serving from .36 to 34 cents which I'm more comfortable with in the long term thank you for watching and if you enjoyed the video don't forget to like share and subscribe see you next time

it has many nutritional benefits and is extremely popular in Japan the smell may be surprising to newcomers but natto is packed with nutrition just eating a 100 gram portion you can enjoy 67 of the daily value of Manganese 48 of iron 27 of magnesium and zinc and also moderate levels of vitamin K calcium potassium and selenium among others natto contains more probiotics than non-fermented So both men and women build bone and peak about up to 25 some sometimes 30 and then bone loss plateaus for a little while. Men continue to lose about 1% a year. So men do not arrive with hip fractures or devastating fractures until their 70s or 80s or older. Women also decline slowly until we lose our estrogen. And then the rate of decline, and I'll tell you why it happens, for bone doubles or triples such that you will lose 15 to 20% of your bone density during your pmenopausal period just from loss of estrogen alone because estrogen plays many roles. I've already talked about estrogen being a huge anti-inflammatory. Bones are very sensitive to inflammatory chemicals in your body. Estrogen also directly affects the balance of the two main cells that subtract minerals from bone, the osteoclast and the cell that builds the bone, the osteoblast. And in well hormoned times, there is a balance. The body loves balance. We take what we need, we build it back. But because estrogen has such a profound effect on the osteoclast, the Pac-Man bone eating cell, there's a dis there's an imbalance and we take more bone than we're capable of building. We're still building bone, but the rate cannot keep up with this. So with the inflammation, with the direct effects on the osteoclass, women will lose bone density if they do nothing. Well, number one, they're going to lose bone density, but they must do something about it. The reason it's so critical for a 33y old is because you still have your estrogen. So, let's learn how to lift heavy. Let's take advantage of the estrogen circulating. Let's learn to have a jumping practice and impact our bones. Right? So the the way our body tells our bones to build more is um there's another little cell called the osteocy. It sits encased in bone but it's connected to these other uh cells by little tunnels that contain fluid. When you jump, the biomechanical impulse of that fluid shifting in the little tunnels is translated into a biochemical stimulation that tells the bone, "Oh my god, she's jumping around. I need to build more bone." So, we need that impact to stimulate the laying down of more bone. >> I was looking at this graph, which is what you were describing. >> Yeah. um men and women aren't that far away in terms of bone mass. >> So this is showing the fact that because of the way testosterone interacts with a man's bones, he will peak at a higher bone mass than a woman will. >> So he's starting at a higher level. >> So I would want young women to do everything humanly possible to start at the highest possible level. And the other thing that isn't really brought up into these conversations is when you hit pmenopause, not only is estrogen sort of the stimulus for sat satellite cell where we're building muscle cell, but it has a distinct um I guess influence on measin. So meosin and actin are are two contractile proteins. So if you think about a sliding filament or coming together, you have meosin and actin that bond together to then pull and move and pull and move and that's how you have a muscle contraction. Estrogen is responsible for how tightly meosin bonds to actin. When we start to lose estrogen or we start to have variability in our estrogen, we get myosin dysfunction. So that means we're not going to get a very strong contraction. >> So we get weaker. >> We get weaker. And this is one of the very first things that people complain about to me. I don't have power. I don't have strength. I can't I my grip strength's gone. I don't know. But their body composition hasn't changed yet. So if we think about lifting heavier loads, it's a neuromuscular response. And that direct stimulus creates the adaptive change in measin that now estrogen isn't responsible for. It's a different external stress that creates that change in measin to go, yep, I have to grab onto actin and have a very strong contraction. So we see that this dysfunction happens first before we start seeing a change in our lean mass development. So if we are already in the habit of lifting heavier loads, then we can offshoot some of that measin dysfunction that occurs with the estrogen change. So when my patients come in and they are looking to prevent osteoporosis or they've gotten a diagnosis of low bone density, you know, what can they do? I've discussed the Lyftmore trial. The new study just came out May of this year of 2025 and they looked at very similar to the protocol used in Lyftmore. So there was a control group where they just did nothing. Then a group that did the they did three days a week and they did resistance training and jump training. And then the third group did all the same exercises and added HRT. And depending if they had a uterus, they got estrogen or estrogen plus a progesterrogen. And so, of course, as expected, the do nothing group lost bone. You gained bone as expected in the resistance group, but it seemed to be synergistic. When we added hormone therapy and the resistance training together, they grew even more bone. They had even better results. So, when my patients are like, I want to hedge my bets. My mother fell and fractured. she's broken her hip in a nursing home, whatever. And I'm trying to avoid this. - Why Keto No Longer Works: Kind: captions Language: en-US This is what a keto diet did for my husband back&nbsp;&nbsp; in 2017. In less than nine&nbsp; months, he lost 81 pounds. Keto worked. In fact, his story&nbsp; was one that was echoed by many&nbsp;&nbsp; back then. Rapid weight loss with&nbsp; inflammatory markers and HbA1c&nbsp;&nbsp; measurements dropping from dangerously&nbsp; high to ideal in a matter of months. Keto success stories are still&nbsp; there but less common nowadays.&nbsp;&nbsp; Did keto suddenly stop working? Did our&nbsp; bodies catch on and say wait a minute,&nbsp;&nbsp; you can't do that anymore. In this&nbsp; video, we will examine what happened&nbsp;&nbsp; to keto and how you can cut through the&nbsp; distractions to recapture the advantage. [Intro Music] In case you are wondering, here is Keith today. Why is he not appearing with me live? Well,&nbsp;&nbsp; we are saving him for a big announcement right&nbsp; after Christmas, so if you are not already,&nbsp;&nbsp; click the subscribe and bell icon so you get&nbsp; a notification of that video of the two of us. Back in 2017, when Keith lost weight&nbsp; by following a ketogenic diet,&nbsp;&nbsp; a device we wish we would have&nbsp; had is a Lumen breath analyzer. While it does not directly measure ketone&nbsp; levels, it analyzes the carbon dioxide&nbsp;&nbsp; you breathe out to determine the fuel&nbsp; your body is running on. Specifically,&nbsp;&nbsp; are you burning fat, carbs,&nbsp; or a combination of the two? Here is how easy it is to use.&nbsp; First thing in the morning,&nbsp;&nbsp; before I eat or drink anything, I open&nbsp; the app and follow the instructions. In seconds, Lumen shows my results&nbsp; in an easy-to-read display that also&nbsp;&nbsp; shares the percentage of fat or&nbsp; carbs that my body is burning. Lumen is scientifically proven to meet the&nbsp; gold standard of metabolism measurement in&nbsp;&nbsp; validation studies. Before Lumen, the only way&nbsp; to measure your metabolism was in a hospital or&nbsp;&nbsp; clinic with a costly and time-consuming test to&nbsp; measure the RER or Respiratory Exchange Ratio. Because Lumen meets this standard,&nbsp;&nbsp; you can feel confident that you are getting&nbsp; actual insights into your metabolism. I can also use Lumen throughout the day,&nbsp;&nbsp; giving me in-the-moment feedback about&nbsp; what my metabolism is burning for energy. There are no filters or anything to restock,&nbsp;&nbsp; so you can test as often as you like, getting&nbsp; unlimited real-time metabolic measurements. If you would like to get a Lumen device for&nbsp; yourself, they are allowing me to share a&nbsp;&nbsp; link that saves you $75. You will find the&nbsp; link by opening the description area below&nbsp;&nbsp; this video. If you are on your phone, click on&nbsp; the word "more" to open the description area. There are two things that I have observed over&nbsp; the past six years that make it seem like keto no&nbsp;&nbsp; longer works. The first is the dilution of keto,&nbsp; from snacks to scare tactics to extremes; today's&nbsp;&nbsp; keto is not the same as it once was. The second is&nbsp; a collective mindset shift, which I will explain. You know, the keto diet is not&nbsp; new. It originated back in the&nbsp;&nbsp; 1920s as a treatment for children with epilepsy. Dr. Atkin's work in the mid-1900s gave&nbsp; the concept of eating a high-fat/low-carb&nbsp;&nbsp; diet a new puff of life at the turn&nbsp; of this century that was then fully&nbsp;&nbsp; revived back around 2016 when the&nbsp; ketogenic diet found social media. Suddenly, the Internet was flooded&nbsp; with success stories and before&nbsp;&nbsp; and after pictures of incredible weight loss&nbsp; results that came about due to nothing other&nbsp;&nbsp; than shopping for and eating different foods&nbsp; from the grocery store. No supplements were&nbsp;&nbsp; needed. No semaglutide "miracle drugs" were&nbsp; needed. In fact, the entry level was barely&nbsp;&nbsp; perceivable. You just put different foods&nbsp; in your grocery cart, and you lost weight. That still works. But we stopped working it. Eating keto meant giving up things like cookies,&nbsp; cakes, and candies. There was no gray area. If you&nbsp;&nbsp; ate a cookie, you were kicked out of ketosis&nbsp; and no longer following a ketogenic diet. Today, we eat keto cookies. When net carbs became a thing, keto cookies&nbsp; and other keto snacks became a thing. And&nbsp;&nbsp; we were more than happy to go along with it&nbsp; because now - suddenly - we could eat cookies. I like cookies. But I know they are not the&nbsp; secret to weight loss. I want them to be. But&nbsp;&nbsp; they are not. They are not today, and they won't&nbsp; be tomorrow. Manipulating the ingredients so&nbsp;&nbsp; 15 total carbs magically turn into 3 net carbs&nbsp; offers you nothing more than an empty promise. You will eat more calories than you need, some&nbsp; of those non-digestible carbs will be digested,&nbsp;&nbsp; and your cravings for more sweet&nbsp; treats will not quiet down. Scare tactics make great headlines, and&nbsp; the word cholesterol can strike fear into&nbsp;&nbsp; the healthiest among us. My husband has&nbsp; a family history of heart disease, so I&nbsp;&nbsp; do not take the topic lightly. LDL cholesterol&nbsp; can increase when you follow a ketogenic diet,&nbsp;&nbsp; and as we all know, LDL carries with&nbsp; it the nickname "bad cholesterol." The question raised by Dr. Paul Mason&nbsp; in this video that I will link to is,&nbsp;&nbsp; what is the evidence that high LDLs will kill you? In his video, he points to this systematic review&nbsp; titled "Lack of an association or an inverse&nbsp;&nbsp; association between low-density-lipoprotein&nbsp; (LDL) cholesterol and mortality in the elderly." Systematic reviews are not looking at one&nbsp; experimental study. Instead, they review&nbsp;&nbsp; multiple studies, looking for patterns. That&nbsp; makes them valuable because you now have a&nbsp;&nbsp; large population of study participants from which&nbsp; to draw conclusions. This review paper assessed&nbsp;&nbsp; more than 68,000 individuals and found that those&nbsp; with the highest LDL levels lived the longest. Our understanding of the human body has&nbsp; grown so much over the past century.&nbsp;&nbsp; Multiple cardiovascular risk factors&nbsp; have been identified, from high HbA1c,&nbsp;&nbsp; triglycerides, and inflammatory&nbsp; markers to LDL particle size. In fact, here is a figure from a paper published&nbsp; in 2022 showing 13 known cardiovascular disease&nbsp;&nbsp; risk factors. Low-carb dieting was shown to&nbsp; be beneficial for 12 of those cardiovascular&nbsp;&nbsp; outcomes, yet we continue to reduce&nbsp; heart health to one factor - LDLs. I am not saying that LDL cholesterol is&nbsp; never part of the problem. It can be,&nbsp;&nbsp; especially if the particles become oxidized&nbsp; or you have too many of them. However,&nbsp;&nbsp; the idea of LDL cholesterol as a single&nbsp; causative factor needs more scrutiny. There are companies with a vested&nbsp; interest in upholding this fear&nbsp;&nbsp; that high LDLs are the end-all-be-all of&nbsp; cardiovascular disease. I encourage you&nbsp;&nbsp; to learn more. I will link to&nbsp; this study, Dr. Mason's video,&nbsp;&nbsp; and my video on cholesterol and low-carb&nbsp; dieting to help you start that learning. If fewer carbs are good,&nbsp; would not no carbs be better? It may be surprising to hear, but your body&nbsp; can run without eating carbohydrates. And&nbsp;&nbsp; this is not just conjecture. This&nbsp; paper, published on NIH's website,&nbsp;&nbsp; states that the dietary carbohydrates you&nbsp; need to consume to stay alive is zero,&nbsp;&nbsp; provided that adequate amounts&nbsp; of protein and fat are consumed. However, you do not need to give up all carbs to&nbsp;&nbsp; follow a low-carb or even keto diet.&nbsp; Some people do. You do not have to. Yet, this idea that keto equals&nbsp; no carb may cause a friend to look&nbsp;&nbsp; at you with concern when you tell&nbsp; them you are starting a keto diet. You can eat a daily salad and have a daily side&nbsp; dish of vegetables with a meal and maintain&nbsp;&nbsp; ketosis. The keto meal plans I provide on my&nbsp; website include them. If you want an easy list&nbsp;&nbsp; to refer to, download my free list of low-carb&nbsp; foods. It gives you 100 foods to choose from. What these aspects of diluting keto - keto&nbsp; snacks, scare tactics, extremes - have done&nbsp;&nbsp; is they have introduced doubt. And when that&nbsp; gets in your head, results cannot happen. In 2017, we were observing keto success&nbsp; everywhere. YouTube, Facebook, Instagram,&nbsp;&nbsp; whatever was around back then was&nbsp; filled with before and after pictures. Doubt is always present, but it was overridden&nbsp; by what you were observing with your own eyes.&nbsp;&nbsp; Those observations created motivational&nbsp; thoughts: "I think I can do this." "Oh&nbsp;&nbsp; my gosh. This is different, but I&nbsp; like those foods." "I can do this." As I said, doubt is always a thing. But back then,&nbsp;&nbsp; our focus was diverted away from doubt&nbsp; to success. What you focus on, you find. Today, those diluting factors we talked&nbsp; about have allowed doubt to trickle back in. "Those keto cookies are everywhere;&nbsp; they can't be that bad. Or at least,&nbsp;&nbsp; they are a step in the right direction.&nbsp; Right?" "I'm going to try them." "I get that LDL cholesterol is not the&nbsp; end-all-be-all of health. But it is scary." "My friend is right. Life is not worth&nbsp; living if I can never eat carbs again!" Our focus slowly shifted from keto works to&nbsp; keto does not work. What you focus on, you find. I do not have all of the answers. I do not know&nbsp; which diet is best for you, and I am not telling&nbsp;&nbsp; you to go keto. My diet is not low enough in carbs&nbsp; to maintain ketosis throughout the week. However,&nbsp;&nbsp; I eat a low-carb diet and practice intermittent&nbsp; fasting, so my body will produce ketones when&nbsp;&nbsp; my glucose level is low and they are needed.&nbsp; That is called being metabolically flexible,&nbsp;&nbsp; and it is a wonderful thing because my&nbsp; body efficiently shifts between running&nbsp;&nbsp; on carbs and running on fat. So, I am never&nbsp; without energy, and I am able to test that. The bottom line of this video is&nbsp; to pick your path, put blinders on,&nbsp;&nbsp; and go. Download my list of 100 low-carb&nbsp; foods. Eat from that list and get a Lumen&nbsp;&nbsp; to see what your body is burning. Are&nbsp; there other low-carb foods? Yes. There are&nbsp;&nbsp; thousands. Are you feeling stuck? Eat from&nbsp; the list, monitor your metabolism, and go. I will provide all of the links mentioned in&nbsp; this video in the description or "more" area&nbsp;&nbsp; below this video. If you would like to get a&nbsp; Lumen breath analyzer, use my link to save $75 I hope this video was helpful. You can do this.&nbsp; It does not matter where you are starting. Please&nbsp;&nbsp; click the subscribe button before you so we&nbsp; can stay in touch and share this video with&nbsp;&nbsp; your community. It means a lot to me when&nbsp; you do so. Have a great rest of your day.

  14. @葉瓊鞠: 整體重點概述
曾有效，但逐漸失靈：
影片一開始提到2017年的真實案例──她的丈夫在九個月內瘦下 81 磅，血糖與發炎指標迅速回復理想值，讓當時許多人見證了 Keto 的神效果。但如今「Keto 成功故事」已不如以往那麼常見，似乎 Keto 不再像以前那般「神奇」
Keto 為何好像不再有效？
Dr. Gillaspy 認為主要有兩大因素：

Keto 零食商品的普及與濫用： 當前市面上所謂「低碳零食」雖宣稱淨碳少，但熱量高又含甜味或加工成分，導致攝入熱量過高、破壞飲食紀律，反而阻礙脂肪燃燒。

膽固醇恐懼心理影響： 因為有人在 Keto 飲食後 LDL（壞膽固醇）增加，引發部分人對 Keto 安全性的懷疑與逃離

飲食心態與「稀釋效應」：
「Keto」一詞變得模糊和寬鬆，人們透過攝取「Keto 零食」、追求極端與迷信網紅口號，使得 Keto 飲食不再純粹，集體認知與飲食行為受此稀釋，導致效果下降


✅ 重點條列
方面	內容
過去 Keto 優勢	快速減重、改善血糖與發炎指標，效果顯著。
導致 Keto 效果下降的主因	◾ 加入過多「Keto 零食」導致熱量過高 ◾ 膽固醇問題引發戒斷 ◾ 飲食認知與心態被稀釋
核心建議	回歸「純 Keto」原則，即限制碳水、不攝取加工 Keto 零食、並維持心態專注。

🎯 總結與建議
Keto 本身仍然可以有效，但關鍵在於：「你吃的是純粹的低碳高脂食物嗎？或是被「Keto 標籤」包裝的加工零食取代了？」

若你追求健康或減肥效果，應該側重於高品質全食物，而非依賴加工 Keto 商品。

同時，保持對脂質變化的監測（尤其 LDL）與飲食心態的穩定，是 Keto 成敗的關鍵。

Dr. Gillaspy 提到的改善策略


我來幫你整理 Dr. Becky Gillaspy 在 〈Why Keto No Longer Works〉 中提到的改善策略（繁體詳細版）：

🔄 Dr. Gillaspy 的 Keto 改善策略
1⃣ 回到「乾淨 Keto」原則
少碰加工「Keto 零食」

不要依賴「零碳餅乾」「Keto 巧克力」等包裝食品，即使淨碳低也可能高熱量、高甜味劑。

❤❤😊😊強調 全食物：肉類、蛋、魚、海鮮、低碳蔬菜、天然脂肪（橄欖油、酥油、牛油果油等）。

2⃣ 避免熱量過剩
即使低碳，也可能熱量超標

許多人 Keto 失敗的原因不是碳水回升，而是熱量攝取過多。

建議：

觀察份量：肉類、乳製品、堅果的攝取不宜無限放開。

偶爾短期減少脂肪攝入，讓身體動用儲存脂肪。

3⃣ 監控膽固醇與血脂
有些人在 Keto 後 LDL 升高，可能因為攝取太多飽和脂肪。

建議：

優先選擇 單元不飽和脂肪（如橄欖油、酪梨、夏威夷果）。

若 LDL 升幅過高，可暫時增加低澱粉蔬菜或低升糖水果，以平衡血脂。

4⃣ 加入間歇性斷食（Intermittent Fasting）
斷食與 Keto 相輔相成：

Keto 幫助控制飢餓，斷食則進一步提升脂肪動員與胰島素敏感性。

建議嘗試：

16/8 時間限制進食法（16 小時斷食，8 小時內進食）。

或 隔日斷食（每週 1–2 次輕度卡路里限制日）。

5⃣ 減少飲食鬆散與心態漂移
Keto 剛開始有效是因為飲食紀律嚴格，後期「習慣」後往往放鬆。

建議：

每隔數週 重新檢視食物內容與分量。

可短期回到 嚴格型 Keto（20g 碳水以下），重新啟動燃脂機制。

👉 總結
Dr. Gillaspy 認為 Keto 沒有效果不是因為 Keto 本身失效，而是執行品質下降。她的核心策略就是 回歸純淨飲食、控制總熱量、監控健康指標、搭配斷食、維持紀律。

- My success story with Katrin Crum: Kind: captions Language: en My husband made me feel like the prettiest girl in the room at 307 pounds. But he was very concerned about my health. I was well on my way to an early death, stroke or heart attack. And I've gone to a lot of different doctors over the years. My doctor said, "You've got to make a change our you're not going to be here much longer." I started on a low-carb path about two years and a half ago. The first thing I did was get off wheat and sugar and that was the beginning point. And then I moved into a pretty traditional low-carb diet. A lot of the reason that prior diets failed for me is because the craving cycle would be so intense, after a little while I would just cave-in. For the first time my appetite started to come under control and in 18 month I lost 150 pounds. I just think there's a big misnomer out there that people that are overweight are just fat and lazy people. That is absolutely not true. And I don't think there's anybody who is morbidly obese and ill who says, "I want to be this way." They just don't know the way. You do have to dig deep at certain points in the journey. 150 pounds is a lot of weight to lose. What I would tell people is just don't give up hope. There is a way to regain your health and reverse the trend of obesity. So now I'm off all medications, my blood pressure is completely normalized, I have no more symptoms of PCOS, I don't have hypertension, I'm no longer pre-diabetic. I am actually healthier now at almost 50 than I was in my 30s and 40s. And so that's what low-carb and keto has done for me. This is one of my pictures after losing 150 pounds. And I actually lost over 100 inches, which is really amazing as well. So I went zip-lining for the first time and snorkeling and walking to the top of the Mayan pyramids and just all of these amazing experiences that I would not have been able to do. Just things like that, just being able to actively participate in life instead of, you know, sitting on the sidelines. It's so much more than the weight loss. The weight loss is a beautiful and wonderful byproduct. But it's being able to regain your health and really be able to fully enjoy your life again.

But, if you are putting out information to the masses my advice is to further your research because you are ill-informing many people which is obviously not a good thing. If you feel what you are doing is benefiting you then good for you but unless you have enough knowledge in biochemistry then you really need to be careful with what information you are giving to others. I am not a doctor nor a physician. But I do have a certain level of knowledge in biochemistry. I have also privately advised and changed the lives of enough people including myself from reiterating science-backed information from clinical research studies that separates fact from fiction. So, a word of advice to everyone. One shouldn't copy someone because they have shown good results at losing weight. Our bodies are different thus tolerate many things differently. What is most important is optimal health. Weight loss will follow as this is the result of a healthy lifestyle. That is why everyone should concentrate on a healthful lifestyle and not losing weight. This sweet young lady is misinformed regarding carbohydrates even though carbs will do its job if this is your main fuel source. However, you will obtain optimal results if your main fuel source is fat. In fact, you will have more energy burning fat because it has over double the amount of calories per gram plus when your cells go through a process called ketogenesis it supressses ghrelin (the hunger hormone) so you will be less hungry as the insulin spikes will be far less and as a result so will inflammation as opposed to carbohydrates being your main fuel source. Your body is very clever and will find balance. So if your main fuel source is fat and the body requires additional glucose the liver will make this via a process called gluconeogenesis - which means generating new glucose. The problem with fuelling your cells with carbs from starch and sugar is that the cells can only accept so much. The receptors will then downregulate. So when these receptors reject insulin (which is made when the body breaks carbs down into glucose) the excess is then stored in your fat cells and also in your bloodstream when the cells are full. You then gain weight and will acquire numerous health problems in your organs and arteries. There is a process called glycation which happens when glucose attaches to proteins (including collagen and elastin proteins) and lipids which is responsible for a number of diseases such as diabetes and is also responsible for chronic inflammation and aging. So, even if you maintain weight loss through rigorous exercise and/or calorie restriction you will still suffer with health problems at some stage due to excess insulin and inflammation. One last note, if you do ever crave bread or a sweet treat/dessert etc it's very easy to make. I love making bread, desserts etc. In fact, one can pretty much eat most of what a western diet consists of, you just have to make everything from scratch with alternative ingredients and have a little patience as opposed to buying already made inflammatory food. Life isn't all sunshine and rainbows. Nobody said it was. It is work. Hard work. But if you plan your work and work your plan it becomes easier and therefore more enjoyable which calls to mind an adage Jim Rohn said which was very apt: "There are two types of pain you will go through in life. The pain of discipline or the pain of regret". I know which one I chose

- My Life Changing 100 Pound Keto Weight Loss Story: Kind: captions Language: en [Music] I don't think anybody knows the sadness and despair being obese causes until they've experienced it. My self-worth was directly tied to my weight since I was 12 years old. I made sure that you knew that I knew I was fat when you met me. Being self-deprecating was my way of being funny, but it also became a part of who I was. If anyone tried to take my picture, I would smile. But inside, I felt so embarrassed about my weight. My smile was not real. If you would have asked me whether or not I would have been fat on my wedding day, I would have said, "Of course not." I dreamed of how I would look on my wedding day, just like everybody else does. But the reality was, I did not lose the weight. I can look back now and accept that I was fat on my wedding day. But I remember feeling extremely disappointed in myself for not following through again. I've tried every diet or gimmick under the sun over the last 23 years to lose weight. I'll admit most of these attempts lasted anywhere from an hour to a couple of months. Regardless of how many times I dieted over the years, I continued to gain weight. Sometimes I would ask myself, "What if I never lose weight? What if I die never experiencing anything other than being obese and out of shape? I've tried keto countless times in the past and I always went back to eating carbs. I know now that I was and am a carb addict. But my twin sister's wedding was motivation for me to try keto again. She was determined to lose as much weight as possible and I didn't want to be obviously bigger than her on her wedding day. So, I started keto again on January 19th, 2019. But this time, I stuck to it. The weight came off slowly compared to how fast I lost weight in the past, but I kept going. I made hard decisions. I said no about a thousand times. My cravings for carbs and sugar started to fade. I learned to cook amazing low carb meals from scratch. I found myself losing anywhere from a pound to even 2 lbs a week. And by the time spring rolled around, I felt motivated to start up old hobbies. I spent time outside for the first time in years. I grew veggies and beautiful flowers in my backyard. I noticed I no longer got hot outside, even when it was 90°. My life was changing. By the time my sister's wedding came around, I weighed in at 189 lb. [Applause] I felt the most confident I ever had before. While I used to hide in pictures, I found myself enjoying having my picture taken at Sarah's wedding. Throughout my weight loss journey, there were several weeks that I lost nothing, but overall, my weight continued to go down. I went from a size extra large to a size small. When I look at myself now, I still can't believe what I'm seeing most of the time. I can actually fit in clothes now and shopping is fun. All I know is for the first time in my life, I feel good about myself. Sure, I still have flaws just like everybody else does, and I still would like to lose another 20 lb, but it's all gravy from here. My starting weight was 262 lb, and today I weigh in at 168 lb. I am no longer obese. My weight is considered normal for the first time in my life. If you're struggling with weight loss right now, just know that if I can do it, anybody can. Just take it one day at a time, one meal at a time. Keep on going. Don't focus on how long it's going to take you to lose the weight. You have to focus on how far you've come already. Momentum builds momentum. Time is going to pass whether you're on this diet or not. But if you stay true to the diet, one day you're going to wake up and you're going to be so far into your ketogenic journey that you're not going to be able to go back to the way that you were living your life before. And the next time someone asks you to smile for a picture, it will be for real.

- 3-Day EATING PLAN to Start or Restart Low Carb Dieting: Kind: captions Language: en-US Whether you got off track or your&nbsp; low carb diet failed to launch,&nbsp;&nbsp; having a plan keeps you from spinning your wheels. This video shares a simple and&nbsp; specific eating plan that you&nbsp;&nbsp; can follow for the next three days&nbsp; to jumpstart your low carb success. [Intro Music] I am often asked to be specific&nbsp; and mention actual foods to eat.&nbsp;&nbsp; The challenge in doing this for a worldwide&nbsp; audience is that the food choices will not&nbsp;&nbsp; work for everyone. If you prefer a plant-based&nbsp; diet or have food restrictions, I am sorry that&nbsp;&nbsp; this video is not for you. However, I have many&nbsp; videos you can benefit from on my channel page. When you switch to a low carb diet, what&nbsp; you do not eat is as important as what you&nbsp;&nbsp; do eat. You want to avoid foods that stimulate&nbsp; your appetite and eat foods that quiet hunger.&nbsp;&nbsp; Let's start with what to avoid. Sugar is quick energy. When you eat it, it&nbsp; gives you an energy boost that feels good.&nbsp;&nbsp; This is one of the reasons eating sugar&nbsp; makes you want to keep eating sugar. The&nbsp;&nbsp; problem is that you cannot survive on&nbsp; a high-sugar diet. Although some of us,&nbsp;&nbsp; including my former self, have given it a good&nbsp; try. At some point, you will not feel good. Ironically, getting away from sugar can&nbsp; also lead to ill feelings. When you go from&nbsp;&nbsp; a high-sugar diet to no sugar, your blood&nbsp; sugar crashes, your energy level plummets,&nbsp;&nbsp; and you may even feel shaky. This happens because&nbsp; the human body runs on what it has available. When&nbsp;&nbsp; sugar is constantly coming in, your body runs on&nbsp; sugar. When you go low carb, you cut out sugar,&nbsp;&nbsp; so it must use an alternate energy source.&nbsp; The good news is that the alternative is fat,&nbsp;&nbsp; so you will become a better fat burner. The&nbsp; temporary bad news is that it will take time&nbsp;&nbsp; for your body to create the enzymes and pathways&nbsp; needed to efficiently burn fat for energy. So,&nbsp;&nbsp; your energy level will drop before it goes up,&nbsp; but this transition is well worth the effort. For the next three days, you want to&nbsp; eliminate added sugar. Specifically,&nbsp;&nbsp; avoid packaged foods and drinks with sugar&nbsp; listed as one of the first three ingredients.&nbsp;&nbsp; That is a long list because it includes nearly&nbsp; all of the ultra-processed foods out there. And&nbsp;&nbsp; unfortunately, ultra-processed foods make up&nbsp; over half of the total calories Americans eat. So, this one rule eliminates a lot of foods,&nbsp; which is okay because those are the foods that&nbsp;&nbsp; make us sick and fat. And you want to keep&nbsp; in mind that sugar goes by many names. So,&nbsp;&nbsp; if the food has corn syrup, dextrose,&nbsp; maltodextrin, fruit juice concentrate, sucrose,&nbsp;&nbsp; or another sugar alias listed as one of the first&nbsp; three ingredients, leave it at the grocery store.&nbsp;&nbsp; If you are unsure if sugar is hidden in a food,&nbsp; assume that it is and leave it at the store. A piece of fruit has natural sugar.&nbsp; That is okay because the sugar is&nbsp;&nbsp; locked within the fibrous portions of&nbsp; the fruit, slowing its absorption. So,&nbsp;&nbsp; you do not have to stop eating fruit of any&nbsp; variety to follow this 3-day eating plan. Two things that you will want to&nbsp; avoid, in addition to added sugar,&nbsp;&nbsp; are bread and breaded foods. You can find&nbsp; bread that has "zero net carbs" listed on&nbsp;&nbsp; the label or no sugar added. However, these&nbsp; commercial products do not deliver on the&nbsp;&nbsp; promise they make. Bread, along with breaded&nbsp; and fried foods, stimulate your appetite,&nbsp;&nbsp; defeating the overarching goal of this preparation&nbsp; period which is to feel in control of your eating. As you get more sophisticated with&nbsp; your low carb diet, you will find&nbsp;&nbsp; ways to make acceptable homemade versions&nbsp; of these foods, but save that until you&nbsp;&nbsp; feel in control of your eating, and you&nbsp; will be much happier with your results. Speaking of homemade. Home cooking&nbsp; is a key to successfully launching&nbsp;&nbsp; or relaunching your low carb&nbsp; diet, and it does not need to&nbsp;&nbsp; be complicated or time-consuming. So&nbsp; let's start building our eating plan. Start by hard-boiling six eggs. You will eat&nbsp; two each day. When you eat them is up to you.&nbsp;&nbsp; If you'd like to have breakfast, eat them in&nbsp; the morning. If you like to shorten your eating&nbsp;&nbsp; window to practice intermittent fasting,&nbsp; you can have them as an afternoon snack. Here again, this plan contains specific&nbsp; foods that do not work for everyone. However,&nbsp;&nbsp; if you can eat eggs, they just are not&nbsp; your favorite, I encourage you to still&nbsp;&nbsp; give this plan a try. The foods I am&nbsp; including were selected because they&nbsp;&nbsp; stabilize your blood sugar and satisfy&nbsp; hunger for hours. That stability has a&nbsp;&nbsp; calming effect, so you do not need to rely&nbsp; on willpower to feel in control of eating. Lunch is a large salad with protein, fat,&nbsp; and some fiber. The reason a salad works&nbsp;&nbsp; so well is because there is a lot to eat,&nbsp; so your stomach is filled with non-starchy&nbsp;&nbsp; vegetables that take a long time to digest. Salad&nbsp; is also the perfect base for adding protein, fat,&nbsp;&nbsp; and fiber. All four of these elements, volume,&nbsp; protein, fat, and fiber, give you sustained&nbsp;&nbsp; hunger control and blood sugar stability, which&nbsp; sets you up for success with your low carb diet. I have a recipe for my typical lunchtime&nbsp; salad, and I will provide a link in the&nbsp;&nbsp; description area below this video.&nbsp; If you just want to build your own,&nbsp;&nbsp; good salad toppers include slices of avocados,&nbsp; raw nuts and seeds, cheese, meat, fish, chicken,&nbsp;&nbsp; and full-fat salad dressing, which can&nbsp; be as simple as olive oil and vinegar. For dinner, you are going for the same&nbsp; hunger-satisfaction effect that you&nbsp;&nbsp; went for at lunch. Therefore, you want to have an&nbsp;&nbsp; entree that contains protein and fat and&nbsp; a side dish that adds volume and fiber. For this plan, make my Easy Keto&nbsp; Chicken Tenders. Meal prep is as&nbsp;&nbsp; easy as putting all of the ingredients in a&nbsp; bag, smooshing them together, and dumping them&nbsp;&nbsp; onto a baking sheet. I will link to the free&nbsp; downloadable recipe in the description area. With that recipe, I share how to make&nbsp; an easy side dish using a bag of frozen&nbsp;&nbsp; broccoli topped with cheese. It is simple&nbsp; to prepare because all you need to do is&nbsp;&nbsp; follow the directions on the bag and add&nbsp; butter and cheese. For that small effort,&nbsp;&nbsp; you add hunger-satisfying volume,&nbsp; fat, and fiber to your meal. You can make life simple by making a double&nbsp; batch of chicken tenders and eating them each&nbsp;&nbsp; day or mix up dinnertime by buying a frozen&nbsp; package of hamburgers and cooking them to have&nbsp;&nbsp; on one or two of the days. Yes, quality matters&nbsp; and grass-fed beef is your best choice, but if&nbsp;&nbsp; that is a barrier for you, look for 100% ground&nbsp; beef burgers so you can get yourself started. The burger will be eaten without a bun. You&nbsp; can wrap it in lettuce leaves if you would&nbsp;&nbsp; like to pick it up and add tomato, onion, and&nbsp; pickles. There are low-sugar ketchup varieties,&nbsp;&nbsp; but you will still be better off going&nbsp; with low-carb condiments, like mustard and&nbsp;&nbsp; real mayonnaise, preferably made with&nbsp; avocado oil, like Primal Kitchen brand.&nbsp;&nbsp; Miracle Whip is not a good choice because&nbsp; it is made with high fructose corn syrup. If you would like a different&nbsp; side dish than broccoli,&nbsp;&nbsp; pick up a bag of green beans and prepare&nbsp; them using the package directions. Will these be the most exciting meals? No. But&nbsp; they taste good, control your blood sugar and&nbsp;&nbsp; hunger, and fill your stomach, which takes&nbsp; us to the next part of our plan, eat enough. We have been conditioned to equate&nbsp; dieting with low calories. Calories&nbsp;&nbsp; are an important consideration for weight&nbsp; loss. However, cutting calories to eat less&nbsp;&nbsp; of the same appetite-stimulating foods&nbsp; leaves you with nothing but misery,&nbsp;&nbsp; and we do not keep doing things that make&nbsp; us miserable, at least not voluntarily. The goal of this 3-day plan is to prepare your&nbsp; body for weight loss. This should be looked at&nbsp;&nbsp; as a time to help your body feel comfortable.&nbsp; That comfort comes when your blood sugar and&nbsp;&nbsp; hunger hormone levels are stable. That stability&nbsp; comes from eating whole foods that have volume,&nbsp;&nbsp; protein, fat, and fiber. If I sound&nbsp; like a broken record, it is intentional. During this 3-day period, eat big portions&nbsp; at mealtimes. Salad fits in this bowl,&nbsp;&nbsp; top it with 4 to 6 ounces of meat, along with the&nbsp; other toppers that I mentioned and that you enjoy. Dinner, if you want an extra portion,&nbsp; eat it. Do not eat dessert. You want to&nbsp;&nbsp; utilize this prep period to move away from&nbsp; the idea that dessert must follow a meal. Those two hard-boiled eggs can be eaten as a&nbsp; small meal before lunch or in the afternoon,&nbsp;&nbsp; so you have three eating occasions during the&nbsp; day. While it might not always be possible,&nbsp;&nbsp; work on eating enough at mealtimes and eliminate&nbsp; snacking in between. But if you need something,&nbsp;&nbsp; I have videos on low carb and keto&nbsp; snacks. A few choices include raw or&nbsp;&nbsp; lightly toasted almonds, cheese,&nbsp; and no-sugar-added beef sticks. Diets often fail because they are&nbsp; a knee-jerk reaction to something.&nbsp;&nbsp; For instance, a friend says, Do you&nbsp; want to go on a diet with me? "Or,&nbsp;&nbsp; you feel uncomfortable in your body&nbsp; and say to yourself, enough is enough." Those moments can initiate action, but if you&nbsp; do not have a plan in place, it is easy to get&nbsp;&nbsp; sidetracked, eat too little, or eat the wrong&nbsp; foods. One day of this uncertainty is okay.&nbsp;&nbsp; Two days can be managed with willpower, but&nbsp; more than that leads to havoc and misery,&nbsp;&nbsp; and the diet fails before&nbsp; the rewards are realized. This 3-day eating plan will get you to the&nbsp; starting line. A well-thought-out low carb&nbsp;&nbsp; diet can get you to the ultimate prize, which&nbsp; is a new way of eating that is easy-to-follow,&nbsp;&nbsp; enjoyable, and effective. When&nbsp; those three E's are in place,&nbsp;&nbsp; there is no need or desire&nbsp; to return to old habits. If you need more motivation and direction&nbsp; to get started, download my free 0,1,2,3&nbsp;&nbsp; strategy. It gives you the basics of the eating&nbsp; plan we just discussed and comes with a video&nbsp;&nbsp; series that is not available on YouTube that&nbsp; explains how to get those three E's in place.&nbsp;&nbsp; You can get those resources by going&nbsp; to DrBeckyFitness.com/Free. Thanks&nbsp;&nbsp; for watching, and have a great rest of your day.

- This Is What a Diabetes-Friendly Diet Looks Like | Diabetes Meal Plan for Beginners | Prep School: Kind: captions Language: en being diagnosed with diabetes can bring on a whole roller coaster of emotions but one thing you don't have to worry about is what to eat we have you covered meal planning and meal prep can go a long way to make healthy eating easier during the busy week and the meal prep muffin tin egg recipe we're going to make today is one of my favorites to make these muffin to omelets are so great because you can make a batch at the beginning of the week grab them from the fridge for breakfast or snacks and they're so delicious so to start we need to whisk some eggs together and we're going to use eight eggs here so eggs as we all know are a great protein Source protein is really important for everyone especially for people with diabetes because protein digests slowly which means our blood sugars will stay more stable after a meal so a single egg delivers six grams of protein per serving and you're wanting to get a few servings of protein on each meal to give you that nice satisfaction that you want at the end of a meal any scrambled egg you need a little milk in here so we're just going to use a quarter teaspoon of salt here so people with diabetes need to be aware of how much salt they're eating in addition to saturated fat those two nutrients are important because they matter to our heart and people with diabetes are at a higher risk for heart related complications and then a quarter teaspoon of pepper and we can start whisking away all right so sometimes we're going to add some nice fresh flavor and we need about quarter of a cup if you don't like chives if you don't have chives you can add some cilantro or scallions or even dried herbs and spices which are nice and convenient Pantry Staples push the chives out of the way here make room for a muffin cup we're going to spray these with a little non-stick cooking spray so they release easily and if you haven't already preheat your oven to 325. okay so here's the trick rather than adding all of your mix-ins into your eggs and then trying to pour it all out evenly a better way of doing this is just to divide your ingredients evenly throughout the muffin cups and this is also a stage where you could do three of one flavor three of another half and half really whatever your heart desires I'm going to start with some broccoli we're just going to evenly distribute this throughout our muffin tin next up ham so ham we know it has some lovely saltiness to it we're just going to use a little bit a little bit goes a long way to add so much flavor this ham also helps to add some protein as well so you're going to get a really nice satisfying breakfast out of this now we move on to our cheese which also adds a little bit of saltiness so we really tested this recipe many times so that we had just the right amount of saltiness without being too much for people with diabetes so this is where the measuring cup comes in handy because you can pour directly out of here so we're just going to evenly distribute this egg mixture throughout don't fill it to the very very top because these eggs really do fluff up and expand once in the oven okay last step here is adding some chives on top for some color and flavor all set to go into the oven 325 degrees Fahrenheit for about 20 to 25 minutes oh here we are 20 minutes in the oven and they are ready we're gonna wait for these egg muffins to cool off a little bit and then you can either pop them in one big meal prep container to go in the fridge or portion them out separately so really all you have to do in the morning is grab your bag and go now this is what a healthy day of eating for a diabetes friendly diet looks like we have our meal prepped muffin tin eggs and a whole day of delicious eats I'm gonna dish out breakfast all it needs to do is be heated up and then enjoyed so for breakfast we have these delicious muffin tin eggs one serving is two muffins and then pairing this with a piece of fruit like a high fiber pear or apple is a great way to add some more sustainability to your meal so this meal plan is set to 1500 calories so for your morning snack you have a nice large handful of almonds almonds are high in heart healthy fat fat is what is satisfying and sustaining so that's going to help carry you through to lunch here we have spinach strawberries or whatever fruit you'd like chicken feta and some walnuts a nice balanced meal paired with a Herby balsamic dressing it's really - Starting Low Carb? Eat This Today | Full Day of Eating: Kind: captions Language: en-US To follow a low carb diet, you must reduce the number of carbohydrate grams you eat in a day. That is an obvious statement, but what does that look like, and what can you eat? In this video, I share a full day of eating a low carb diet for those who are just getting started. [Intro Music] There are many things that cause confusion when you start a low carb diet. One of the main issues is how many grams of carbs to eat? There is low carb dieting, and there is keto dieting. The difference is the level of carbohydrates. While the cut-off points are not fully agreed upon, you can think of a low carb diet as a day of eating that does not contain more than 125 total grams of carbohydrates. To be in the keto range, you need to drop your carb grams to no more than 50 total carbs for the day. For this video, I am going to show you a daily diet that splits those values. These foods in front of me add up to 75 total grams of carbohydrates. One way to start your low carb day of eating is with a smoothie that contains low-sugar fruits with a good fiber-to-carb ratio. Avocado and strawberries fit those criteria. For instance, to make this smoothie, I placed half of an avocado and a half cup of frozen strawberries into a blender along with a cup of almond milk, a handful of spinach, and a tablespoon of almond butter. And, I will share the ingredients for each of these foods in the video. You can also get the recipes on my blog post that goes with this video. I will share a link to my blog below the video in the description area. This smoothie contains 23 grams of carbs, but almost 10 of those carbs are from fiber. That is a good fiber to carb ratio. That fiber is going to slow the absorption of the smoothie, keeping hunger away longer. The slow absorption will also prevent the blood sugar and insulin spike you get when you eat something refined for breakfast, like a muffin. When insulin is high, it prevents the release of fat from the fat cells. So, right from the start of the day, you have a convenient food that tastes good and keeps your blood sugar steady. As you are stepping into your low carb lifestyle, there is no better meal to include than a salad, and there are a couple of great reasons for that. For one, salad greens are low in carbs but provide a lot of hunger-satisfying volume. For instance, this salad has about four cups of greens but only six carbs, four of which are fiber. Also, a salad provides you with the perfect vehicle to bring healthy fats into your daily diet. One of the biggest mental hurdles you will face when you shift to low carb eating is allowing yourself to increase the amount of fat you eat. We were told for a year that eating fat makes you fat, and it is hard to get away from that mindset. However, adding healthy, high-quality fats to your diet helps your body transition away from a primarily carb-burning metabolism to a fat-burning metabolism. This salad is topped with many healthy fats including, the other half of the avocado from breakfast, a hard-boiled egg, feta cheese crumbles, walnuts, and a full-fat salad dressing made with avocado oil. That adds up to 36 grams of fat. The salad also contains a few slices of apple that provide a crunch and a bit of sweetness. The apple and greens contribute most of the 22 grams of carbohydrates and 9 of fiber in this lunchtime salad. For dinner, stir fry is a quick and easy choice for beginners, especially if you utilize frozen stir fry vegetables and a pre-cooked rotisserie chicken from the grocery store. To make this, I simply cooked a bag of frozen vegetables in a tablespoon of avocado oil, added the pre-cooked chicken and a quick homemade sauce. One thing that may surprise you when you start a low carb diet is that many sauces and condiments contain sugar and high-fructose corn syrup. Those simple carbs can sabotage your weight loss. Fortunately, you can often whip up a homemade, low-carb version. For instance, this stir fry sauce contains a quarter cup of chicken broth, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon of red wine vinegar. As you transition to a low carb lifestyle, yogurt is something that can be enjoyed as a dessert or snack. However, you need to choose yogurt carefully. The vast majority of single-serve yogurts in the dairy aisle of your grocery store are low in fat. When they removed the fat, they removed the flavor, which they replace with sugar or sweeteners. To support your low carb lifestyle, you will want to choose plain, full-fat yogurt and then add your own ingredients at home. Higher milk fat yogurt may only be available in larger containers, but they keep well in your refrigerator, even after opening. This yogurt dessert contains a quarter cup of frozen blueberries that I microwaved for 30 to 45 second and then pressed to extract the juices. I then added a half cup of yogurt, a half teaspoon of vanilla, and a pinch of pure Stevia. Now, This video is being made for YouTube, which is a broad audience. The meals in front of me add up to just over 1,200 calories, which will be low for some, but this works well for demonstration purposes because it is easier to add low-carb/high-fat items rather than subtract them. When we breakdown the macros for this day of eating, we see that it contains 75 total grams of carbohydrates, making up 25 percent of the daily calories with another 25 percent coming from protein and just over 50 percent of the day's calories coming from healthy fats. Many of you will find this macronutrient breakdown to be a comfortable starting place if you are coming from a high-carb lifestyle. It allows you to step into a low carb diet rather than dive into a very low carb/keto When you start this way, you may still experience some fatigue as your body adapts, but lessen many of the "keto-flu" symptoms that result from a more dramatic change. Now, I would like to draw your attention to a couple of things about this low carb menu in front of me. First of all, there are three small servings of fruit: blueberries, strawberries, and slices of apple. When you are just getting started on your low carb lifestyle, you may find that your body tolerates some fruit. However, everyone's tolerance for fruit is different, so you be happiest with your long-term results if you limit your fruit intake. I have many videos that discuss fruit on my channel if you would like to explore that topic further. You will also notice that there is no bread item like toast or a bagel. A slice of whole-wheat toast contains about 16 grams of carbohydrates. If you are looking to bring your carb intake below the upper threshold of 125 grams, you could technically include a piece of toast in your diet. However, bread is refined, so it will get absorbed into your bloodstream quickly, causing the blood sugar and insulin spikes that encourage fat storage. There ARE breads that are advertised as low-carb or no-carb. This is accomplished by manipulating the ingredients to reduced the net carb count. On paper, that seems ideal. However, these processed foods tend to be absorbed into the body quickly, and depending on what has been added, may cause an increase in blood sugar and insulin. When processed foods are utilized, counting net carbs rather than total carbs can easily become a trap that derail your weight loss. That is why I am sharing the total carb content for this low carb menu. You will also notice that this menu is made up of whole foods. There are some whole foods that are higher in carbs that can be worked into a beginner's low carb diet due to their high content of fiber and micronutrients. For instance, this salad does not contain beans. However, if you are just moving your diet into the low-carb range, you can make them work. Beans are whole, nutrient-dense foods, but they also contain a fair amount of carbohydrates. To give you an idea, a half cup of black beans contains about 22 grams of carbs and nine grams of fiber. If you account for those carbohydrates and you enjoy beans, you can work them into your overall daily diet. I hope you see from this video that a low-carb diet made up of whole foods is enjoyable and effective for weight loss. I provided a few examples here, but there are many more healthy low-carb foods to keep you feeling satisfied. I have a list of 100 low carb foods displayed on a single pdf that you can download and print for free from my website. I will provide links to the food list here on the video and below in the description area. Please subscribe to my channel and hit the bell icon to get notified when my new videos are released. Thanks for watching and enjoy the rest of your day.
